title: Manufacturing Engineer (Plastic) #79721
raw_description:
Job Description

  • Industry/ Organization Type: Manufacturer for Semi-Con Industry
  • Position Title: Manufacturing Engineer
  • Working Location: Loyang
  • Working Hours: 5 days (Mon-Fri: 8am-5.30pm)
  • Salary Package: Basic + Variable Bonus
  • Duration: Permanent

Key Responsibilities

  • Help develop manufacturing process plans for new and existing products.
  • Support manufacturing engineers to review production cycle times and identify improvements.
  • Help design cutting tools, fixtures, and gauges for production and inspection.
  • Work with the production team to troubleshoot and solve technical issues.
  • Learn to perform cost and time analysis for different manufacturing processes.
  • Assist with first article (FA) preparation and documentation under supervision.
  • Take part in continuous improvement projects, including safety, quality, and cost reduction.
  • Training and Development:-
  • On-the-job training and mentorship from experienced engineers.
  • Exposure to industry-standard CAD/CAM tools (e.g., SolidWorks, SolidCAM).
  • Build skills in project management, problem-solving, and technical documentation.

APPLY NOW!!!

  • Diploma or degree in Manufacturing Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or a related field.
  • Strong interest in manufacturing processes, production planning, and continuous improvement.
  • Basic CAD knowledge is an advantage (training will be provided).
  • Familiar with Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, etc.).
  • Willing to learn, adapt, and work both independently and as part of a team.
  • Good communication and people skills.
  • Strong analytical skills with attention to detail.

Kindly apply through ANY of the following methods:

  • Submit your application by clicking the APPLY button;
  • Email your resume to Job@anradus.com.sg. Please indicate #79721 on the email subject.

Anradus Application Policy: We value each application and ensure every resume is reviewed. Our process is efficient, typically concluding within 3 working days. If you do not receive communication from us within this timeframe, it's likely that your application has not been shortlisted by our client. In such cases, we recommend continuing your job search to maximize your opportunities.

title: Banking Sales Associate
raw_description:
Responsibilities:


1. Customer Engagement:

- Build and maintain strong relationships with customers.

- Provide excellent customer service and address inquiries regarding financial products and services.

- Actively engage in cross-selling and upselling financial products to meet sales targets.


2. Product Knowledge:

- Stay updated on the bank's products and services.

- Effectively communicate product features and benefits to customers.

- Assist customers in selecting the right banking products to meet their financial needs.


3. Sales Targets:

- Achieve and exceed monthly sales targets.

- Identify opportunities for revenue generation and actively pursue them.

- Collaborate with team members to enhance overall sales performance.


4. Documentation and Processing:

- Assist customers in completing necessary documentation for various financial transactions.

- Ensure accuracy and compliance with financial regulations in all customer interactions.

- Process the forwarding of account openings, loan applications, and other financial transactions efficiently.


5. Compliance:

- Stay informed about and adhere to all relevant banking/ financial regulations and compliance standards.

- Ensure that all customer interactions and transactions comply with internal policies and external regulations.


6. Problem Resolution:

- Address and resolve customer issues and complaints in a timely and effective manner.

- Collaborate with other departments to resolve complex customer inquiries.


7. Team Collaboration:

- Work closely with team members to achieve overall goals.

- Participate in team meetings and contribute to a positive team environment.


8. Market Research:

- Stay informed about industry trends, market conditions, and competitor offerings.

- Provide feedback to management regarding customer preferences and market demands.


9. Training and Development:

- Participate in ongoing training programs to enhance product knowledge and sales skills.

- Share insights and best practices with team members to improve overall performance.



Qualifications:

- Local diploma or A Levels; bachelor's degree in business or related field is a plus.

- Strong interpersonal and communication skills.

- Knowledge of banking products and services.

- Ability to work in a target-driven environment.

- Attention to detail and strong organizational skills.

title: Research Assistant (Mapping Population Preferences)
raw_description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS Career Portal

Your application will be processed only if you apply via NUS Career Portal

We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Job Description

Job Advertisement: Research Assistant 

Workpackage 4 – Mapping Population Preferences


The Saw Swee Hock School of Public Health (SSHSPH) at the National University of Singapore is inviting applications for a Research Assistant to contribute to Workpackage 4 of the Personalised Outcomes in Populations for Upstream Prevention (POPUP) project. This workpackage seeks to understand how Singaporeans perceive and engage with different modes of preventive healthcare delivery—ranging from digital tools to traditional face-to-face services—and how these preferences vary across demographic and socioeconomic groups.

Assist in designing and administering population preference surveys and qualitative interviews

Key Responsibilities

Assist in designing and administering population preference surveys and qualitative interviews

Qualifications

Familiarity with survey methods or qualitative research techniques

Desirable Attributes


- Prior experience with mixed-methods research or patient preference studies
- Familiarity with qualitative data software (e.g. Atlas.ti) and/or statistical packages (e.g. R or Stata)
- Experience in community health settings or working with underserved populations
- Interest in population health, digital health, and health equity

Terms of Appointment


- Full-time position based in Singapore
- Initial appointment for 12 months, with the possibility of extension
- Salary will be competitive and commensurate with qualifications and experience

To Apply


Please send the following documents during application: 

1. A cover letter outlining your background, motivation, and fit for the role (max 500 words)
2. A two-page CV including the names and contact details of two referees
3. A writing sample (e.g. academic paper, policy brief, or analytic report)

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an interview.

title: PART TIME ASSISTANT ART TEACHER
raw_description:
ART TEACHING ASSISTANT @ LyttleSpace (Bedok South)
PART TIME POSITION STARTING FEB/MARCH
(2-3 days work week. At least 1 weekday and 1 weekend)
(Must be able to commit at least 6 months.)
Weekday hours: Tuesday-Friday 2-6.30pm
Weekend hours: Sat-Sun 9am-6.30pm

Looking to hire a part time merry maker to join our cosy team!

At LyttleSpace, every child is celebrated as a unique individual, and we create a supportive community to elevate their creative expressions. As an assistant teacher, you will dive into the fun of class preparations, guiding little ones in their artistic adventures, and fostering a community of creative makers. If you love hands-on involvement, to be a part of lesson planning and creative prototype making and be in a dynamic environment full of magic making, this is the place for you. Working with kids is exhausting but can be very rewarding! Part time position with fixed hours. Being able to commit to the hours is important to keep the sense of familiarity for the kids too.

POSITION OVERVIEW:
Assistant teacher will help support in preparation and facilitation of classes. You will engage in the learning and development of the students, encouraging them to be creative and imaginative. We adopt a child led approach and practice respectful teaching.

There may be extra sessions during school holidays and birthday parties. You will help to plan, prepare and assist. You will also assist in setting up and clean up before and after classes.

QUALITIES WE VALUE:

  • Child-Led Approach— Our ideal candidate understands the importance of genuine care when working with young children. Respect, patience, and a positive attitude are not just desirable, but essential qualities for fostering a nurturing environment. Embracing our child-led teaching philosophy to inspire creativity and imagination.
  • Growth Mindset — We appreciate individuals who are open to learning and growing. A willingness to embrace new teaching methodologies, along with a growth mindset and the ability to accept constructive feedback, is vital in our collaborative space.
  • Strong Communication Skills — Clear communication is key. Proficiency in English, both written and spoken, enables effective interaction with both our young artists and their families.
  • A Collaborative, Hands On Environment — Experience the joy of working in a warm and collaborative atmosphere where creativity thrives. There is alot of hands-on involvement with prototype making, fostering a dynamic and interactive learning environment
  • Self Artistic Exploration — Explore your own artistic expressions while encouraging children to explore theirs. We believe that an essential aspect of being a good teacher is not only guiding children on their creative journey but also embarking on an art practice of your own. It will be a source of inspiration and understanding of your very own creative process.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Genuine love working with young children is a must.
- Adaptable to change in a quick working environment.
- Respectful
- Patient with children
- Positive attitude
- Willingness to learn
- Have a growth mindset and open to feedback.
- Good command of English
- Good social skills
- Organised
-Able to commit at least 6 months

QUALIFICATIONS:
Current students or fresh grads with Diploma in Fine Arts/Design/Art Education, pursuing degree or Pre-U/A level/College art stream or related discipline. Experience in early childhood and conducting art classes preferred but not a must.

SALARY:
$11 per hour
1 month probation
Salary will be adjusted on a yearly basis, or based on performance.

ADDRESS:
Blk 158 Bedok South Ave 3
Singapore 460158

Interested candidates who can commit to the hours above.
Kindly email your portfolio and resume to lyttlespace@gmail.com with the subject title
APPLYING FOR PART TIME POSITION - ART TEACHING ASSISTANT
Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Job Types: Part-time, Student job

Pay: $11.00 per hour

Education:

  • Local Polytechnic Diploma (Preferred)

Work Location: In person

title: Physics Tuition Teach
raw_description:
About Axiom Education

Axiom Education is a leading tuition centre for IB, A-Level (H2), Integrated Program (IP) and O-Level. We pair rigorous, structured teaching with a fun, competitive classroom vibe. With proven materials, engaging methods and a vibrant, high-energy learning community, our students consistently climb to 7s and As.


The Role

Teach O level, IP, IB & H2 Physics full-time. Run interactive, exam-focused classes and mentor students

Ideal for an educator with 3–5 years’ strong results.


What You’ll Do

  • Deliver engaging lessons aligned to IB & H2 Physics syllabus
  • Create notes, practice sets, and model answers; track progress with clear feedback.
  • Coach exam technique (timed practice, structured problem-solving, marking criteria).
  • Support students beyond class with mentorship and guidance.
  • Stay current with syllabus updates and exam trends.

What You Bring

  • 3–5 years proven IB/H2 teaching or tutoring experience in Physics with strong outcomes.
  • Degree in Physics or related field.
  • Mastery of core topics and problem-solving strategies.
  • Clear communicator; organised, proactive, high-energy, student-centric.

Compensation (Full-Time)

  • Revenue-share on Net Class Fees with significant upside as your classes scale.

Location

  • Holland Village
  • Balmoral Plaza

Apply Now

Send your resume to info@axiomeducation.sg

title: Sourcing Officer - WCAN
raw_description:
Responsibilities:

  • Oversee the full procurement lifecycle, including sourcing local and international suppliers, negotiating contracts, and issuing purchase orders.

  • Develop and implement procurement strategies to optimize costs and enhance operational efficiency.

  • Conduct market research to identify and evaluate potential suppliers based on quality, pricing, and reliability.

  • Manage company facilities, logistics, and shipping operations for both local and international activities.

  • Ensure compliance with company policies, legal regulations, ISO standards, and HSE requirements.

  • Administer all company licensing, insurance renewals, and vehicle maintenance and registration.

  • Resolve supplier disputes and address issues related to delivery, quality, or performance.

  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support project planning and procurement requirements.

  • Monitor inventory levels, maintain accurate records, and forecast future needs to ensure optimal stock.


Qualifications:

  • Diploma or Degree in Procurement, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Engineering, or a related discipline.

  • Minimum 3 years of practical procurement experience, preferably within M&E, construction, engineering, facilities management, or manufacturing industries.

  • Excellent negotiation, communication, and interpersonal skills.

  • Demonstrated ability to manage multiple procurement projects concurrently.

  • Detail-oriented with strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

  • Capable of working independently or collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.

  • Solid understanding of Singapore’s procurement regulations and industry best practices.

title: Assistant Accountant
raw_description:
Position Overview: We are looking for a reliable and detail-oriented Accounts Assistant to support the Finance and Admin (F&A) team with day-to-day accounting operations. This role involves a variety of tasks across accounting functions, requiring good organizational skills, accuracy, and the ability to meet deadlines.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Cash Book & Bank Reconciliation: Maintain and update cash book daily; reconcile with bank statements and resolve discrepancies.
  • Accounts Receivable (AR): Update AR Aging Report; prepare and issue monthly SOAs; coordinate monthly AR meetings and follow-up.
  • Accounts Payable (AP): Verify supplier invoices and payment vouchers with supporting documents.
  • Invoicing & Notes: Upload invoices and credit/debit notes to the accounting system; prepare DN/CN and intercompany billing.
  • Petty Cash: Manage petty cash disbursements, documentation, and weekly reconciliation.
  • GST Filing: Assist in GST preparation and ensure compliance.
  • Stock Take: Assist in monthly stock take, include counting and recording and reconciliation.
  • Fixed Assets: Maintain fixed asset register and support asset tagging and disposal processes.
  • Journal Vouchers: : Prepare journal vouchers monthly
  • Balance sheet Schedule: Update the balance sheet monthly
  • Road Tax and Insurance: Assist in Road Tax and Insurance Renewal
  • Audit Support: Provide necessary documentation and assist in internal and external audits.
  • General Support: Provide support to the team or cross-functional support during month-end closing
  • Others: Perform additional responsibilities and tasks assigned, which may extend beyond the scope of the duties outlined above

Accounting System: Sage 300

Requirements:

  • Singaporean / PR
  • Diploma / Degree in Accounting or related field
  • 1–2 years of relevant experience
  • Proficiency in MS Excel and accounting software (e.g., Accpac)
  • Strong attention to detail, able to multitask and meet deadlines
  • Good communication and team player

title: Junior Art Educator
raw_description:
Job Description & Requirements

Art Wonderland is an art education studio that provides children of all ages with a fun, warm and nurturing environment to learn art. We engage children through a variety of art mediums and techniques, and connect their learning in the classroom to their everyday environment and experiences.

We are looking for a bubbly, passionate and self-driven art educator that seeks to inspire our young minds through art, to be future creative thinkers.

Responsibilities:

  • Research, conceptualise and design lesson curriculums for ages 2 - 12 years old
  • Leading and teaching studio classes, school programmes with museum partners and external workshops and events
  • Involves working on weekends for classes and events
  • Explore and conceptualise groundbreaking art programmes

We are looking for an art educator that:

  • Loves to work with children and seeks to inspire them
  • Is creative and takes risks, but also well prepared for any situations that arises in the classroom
  • Who agrees with the values and benefits of inquiry-based discussions during art lessons to bring out the creative thinkers in children
  • Seeks out new topics and subjects to turn into a lesson to share with children
  • Looking to be surrounded by other creatives that believe strongly in art education and bringing art closer to children and parents

Essential Qualifications:

  • Fresh Graduates are welcome to apply
  • Diploma either in Fine Arts or Art Education
  • Must love to work with children and comfortable speaking to an audience
  • Must have experience with traditional art mediums
  • A plus if you have any experience teaching and interacting with children aged 2 - 12 years old

title: Livestream Sales Admin
raw_description:
Job Title: Channel Executive (open part time and full time)

Report to: Channel Manager

Location: Singapore & Overseas


Role Responsibilities

· Support Channel Manager to manage/create and grow our brand channel.

· To provide administrative support to live-streamers during their live shows with customer support and payments administration.

· Establish clear brand channel strategy and positioning and marketing of the channel using FB and IG or other social media.

· Travelling for overseas streams.

· Planning calendar of sales events (Special stream or events).

· Support end to end operations and logistics and fulfilment for the brand channel.

· Posting and planning of social media contents.

· Review and analyse live stream data.

· Doing up daily/ weekly/monthly reports and KPI for the channel.

· Reconcile and prepare of delivery reports after livestream shows.

· Support sourcing new Merchants and live streamers.

· Merchants & Streamers Management.

· Customer Service.

· Other tasks as assigned by the hiring manager.


Opportunities/Progression

Great learning opportunity: As start-up company, we offer ample opportunity for you to learn things that you probably wouldn't be exposed to in other corporate organisation. Additionally, you will have direct access to the people behind the start-up and will be able to learn from them every step of the way. You will learn more about business strategy, marketing and sales, operations from the top management team.


Overseas Opportunities: There are tons of overseas opportunities and the potential to change the landscape of Livestreaming locally or overseas.


Competitive Career Path: Working in our company often experience more job satisfaction because you are typically more involved in the growth and evolution of the company. You would be promoted to Channel Manager; other functional directors’ opportunity is also available. The CEO has developed great excellent leaders in the company.


Qualification & Experience

· Minimum Diploma/degree holders, major in business management, marketing, communications etc. would be preferable but not a must.

· Retail experience is preferred.

· Intermediate Excel understanding and skill preferred.

· Holding a driving licence and able to drive in overseas. (MUST)

· Able to cope with fast work pace and meticulous to details.

· Candidates are required to be bilingual; livestream shows are done in both Chinese and English.

· Must be prepared to travel overseas (50%) for livestream shows when required.

· Willingness to work occasional overtime to accommodate various time zones.


About us

LiveCommerce Entertainment – a B2B Marketplace connecting Brands and Live Streamers to Start Live Selling on FB and IG Live. Live selling on FB/IG became a new way for people to stay engaged and entertained as they tuned in to Live Selling shows and connected and bonded with other viewers and hosts of the live shows.

Our mission is to Excite & Entertain by providing our customers Live shopping entertainment! We aim to build a B2B marketplace platform for brands and Live streamers that will dominate social commerce in Asia. Our company values continuous learning, inquisitiveness, being versatile and adaption as well as creating an exciting work environment.

raw_description:

IT Infrastructure

  • Work closely with management committee in ensuring the best IT Infrastructure in place for Muhammadiyah Association as a whole.
  • Oversee all IT functions, including network administration, system administration, hardware/software maintenance, cybersecurity, and data management.

Additional duties

  • Degree/Diploma in IT, Computer Science or related studies with 2 to 3 or more years of working experience
  • Positive attitude with a team-player mindset with good communications and problem-solving skill

title: Piano Teacher
raw_description:
Piano Teacher – Full-Time, Permanent

5-Day Work Week | 2 Days Off


Why Join LVL Music Academy?

  • Singapore’s first all-in-one music hub: music school, instrument shop, and in-house repair workshop under one roof
  • Specialising in string instruments (violin, cello & piano) for students of all ages
  • Supportive, inspiring, and professional environment where teachers and students thrive

About the Role

LVL Music Academy is seeking a passionate and experienced Piano Teacher to deliver high-quality music instruction and nurture students’ musical development. The ideal candidate will have strong organisational and teaching skills, deep product knowledge, and a love for music education, capable of promoting LVL’s services and programs effectively.


Your Role:

  • Deliver engaging piano lessons to students aged 3.5 years to adults, tailored to individual learning needs.
  • Plan, prepare, and execute structured lesson plans to ensure consistent progress
  • Follow LVL's cirriculum and guides (full training will be provided)
  • Teach music theory (ABRSM) and prepare students for examinations.
  • Organise and manage recitals and performance opportunities to showcase student achievements.
  • Maintain clear, proactive communication with students and parents to support learning outcomes.
  • Collaborate with fellow teaching staff to enhance curriculum and student experience.

What We’re Looking For:

  • Minimum Diploma in Music, with a degree preferred.
  • At least 2 years of teaching experience, with a proven track record in piano instruction.
  • Proficient in teaching both practical piano skills and music theory to students of all ages.
  • Strong organisational skills with the ability to plan and oversee recitals.
  • Collaborative, positive, and passionate about nurturing students’ musical journeys.

Perks and Packages

  • Base Salary: $4,200 – $5,000 per month
  • Medical and Insurance Coverage (Hospital & Surgical)
  • 14 Days Annual Leave & Sick Leave
  • In-House Learning, Development, and Training
  • Professional Career Development Opportunities
  • Complimentary Music Lessons
  • Vibrant and Supportive Company Culture
  • Opportunity to travel overseas with the team for annual performances
  • Performance incentives and continuous training opportunities

About LVL Music Academy

Founded in 2013, LVL is Singapore’s leading music school and instrument shop, specialising in string instruments (violin, cello & piano). We are the first music hub in Singapore to combine a music school, instrument shop, and in-house repair workshop under the same roof, providing students, parents, and musicians with a truly comprehensive music experience.

Ready to Inspire the Next Generation of Musicians?
Submit your resume and cover letter now!

title: Helpdesk Support Engineer
raw_description:
Position Overview

We are seeking a dedicated IT Support Specialist to join our team and ensure seamless technology operations across our organisation. This role combines technical expertise with excellent customer service skills to support end-users and maintain critical IT infrastructure across multiple locations on Sentosa Island.


Key Responsibilities


Infrastructure & Network Support

  • Maintain and support end-user computing devices (EUCDs) and network infrastructure, including LAN and WiFi systems.
  • Oversee back-end server operations to ensure optimal performance and reliability across all locations.

Security Management

  • Administer and manage anti-virus solutions, specifically Trend Micro and Apex One Office Scan systems.
  • Ensure all IT systems remain current with security patches for Windows, Intel drivers, and applications to maintain organisational security standards.

Service Desk Operations

  • Provide comprehensive helpdesk support through multiple channels including email, IT hotline, walk-in requests, and both onsite and remote assistance.
  • Maintain detailed logs of all incidents and support requests whilst ensuring timely resolution through our ITSM platform.

Technical Support & Deployment

  • Manage front-end client systems running Windows 11 and other operating systems, including imaging, deployment, and troubleshooting.
  • Provide technical assistance for hardware and software setup, configuration, and problem resolution.

Asset & Vendor Management

  • Maintain accurate IT asset management records, including asset tagging and proper disposal of equipment.
  • Coordinate with vendors for system setup, repairs, and maintenance activities whilst logging all cases appropriately.

Documentation & Reporting

  • Create clear technical documentation and user manuals with step-by-step instructions.
  • Maintain work-in-progress reports and ensure all activities are properly documented for knowledge sharing and compliance purposes.

Additional Support

  • Configure and support online video conferencing systems to facilitate effective communication across the organisation.
  • Undertake other duties as assigned by the IT Supervisor to support overall departmental objectives.

Required Qualifications

  • Diploma or degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field
  • Minimum 2-3 years of experience in IT support or helpdesk roles
  • Strong knowledge of Windows operating systems, particularly Windows 11
  • Experience with network troubleshooting (LAN/WiFi)
  • Familiarity with ITSM platforms and ticketing systems
  • Knowledge of anti-virus management systems (Trend Micro preferred)
  • Understanding of hardware and software troubleshooting methodologies

Preferred Qualifications

  • Professional certifications (CompTIA A+, Network+, or Microsoft certifications)
  • Experience with server administration
  • Knowledge of video conferencing systems
  • Asset management system experience
  • Previous experience in government or public sector environments

Key Competencies

  • Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills
  • Strong communication and interpersonal abilities
  • Customer service orientation with focus on user satisfaction
  • Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities
  • Attention to detail and commitment to documentation
  • Willingness to travel between multiple office locations
  • Adaptability to changing technology environments

title: Engineer, Failure Analysis, NTI
raw_description:
Micron Technology is a world leader in innovating memory and storage solutions that accelerate the transformation of information into intelligence, inspiring the world to learn, communicate and advance faster than ever!

We need someone who is passionate about detecting problems, making changes for the better, and seek out innovative ways in how predict device performance! We are an equal opportunity company who values of People, Innovation, Customer Focus, Tenacity and Collaboration.

As Yield Failure Analysis Engineer in Micron NAND Technology Integration (NTI) Singapore department, you will be responsible for providing yield analysis for next-generation Micron products. In this exciting role, you will become a domain expert on physical failure analysis methods for any given electrical failure! You will also participate in the identification and communication of yield issues, assist with the root cause analysis for process deviations, characterize, probe, parametric, and fabrication data to identify the sources of failures.

Job Responsibility:

  • Perform Failure analysis which includes conducting electrical characterization, perform electrical verification to confirm failures, and understanding PFA techniques available for de-processing failures

  • Drive failure analysis initiatives for the yield enhancement team. Create and deliver new methodologies, capabilities, and innovative ways for failure analysis de-processing failures

  • Participate in global meetings and present results to Product Development and Process Integrations teams

  • Collaborate with metrology and RDA team to re-define and validate inline detection capability

  • Assist and support the transfer documentation of layout/architecture fundamentals of new technology from R&D to manufacturing fab.

  • Understand and follow all health, safety, and environmental procedures and requirements.

Requirement:

  • Bachelor of Science or Master Degree in Electrical Engineering, Microelectronics, Material Science, Mechanical Engineering.

  • 4 - 7 years experience in Failure Analysis or PFA, with includes conducting electrical characterization, perform electrical verification to confirm failures, understanding PFA techniques available for de-processing failures

  • Fluency in Python, JavaScript, C or C++ is Windows, Linux, JMP statistic software is desirable.

  • Drive failure analysis initiatives for the yield enhancement team. Create and deliver new methodologies, capabilities, and innovative ways for failure analysis de-processing failures

    Develops new or modified process formulations, defines processing or handling equipment requirements and specifications, and reviews processing techniques and methods applied in the manufacture, fabrication, and evaluation of products. Involvement may begin at any step from pilot plant to full-scale manufacturing. Coordinates design requirement review with appropriate engineering/scientific personnel to ensure compatibility of processing methods. Compiles and evaluates test data to determine appropriate limits and variables for process or material specifications. May conceive and plan projects involving definition and selection of new concepts, equipment automation technology, and approaches in the processing or development of new or improved processes.

title: Product Development Engineer
raw_description:
THE ROLE: 

As a Product Development engineer, you will collaborate with various Product Development Groups to bring-up and ramp new products which include Plan, debug and handling manufacturing and characterization test programs for next-generation AMD microprocessors. 

THE PERSON: 

A successful candidate is one who can perform in a role that requires accountability for accuracy, reliability and completeness of assignment results critical to the project’s success.  

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 

  • Validate new product features, test conditions, test methodologies, and test contents during first silicon bring-up 
  • Post Silicon Bring up of Test patterns leading to optimization for mass production enablement 
  • Analysis of part failures leading to test improvement 
  • Test pattern & test flow development, debug, test and characterization 

PREFERRED EXPERIENCE:

title: Teacher (Part / Full - Time)
raw_description:
Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition

Welcome on board the Happy Train, where the journey of learning is always fun and exciting! The Happy Train programme is a Holistic brain-training programme that enriches children’s growing minds through a variety of games, guided play and hands-on activities. The curriculum is designed to motivate the little learner’s aspiration of achievement. Our hand-made teaching materials encourage children to explore concepts and ideas through experiential learning. We believe in the importance of developing core skills and shaping children’s learning attitudes. We are the developer of the unique multi-lingual brain development programme for children aged 3 months to 6 years old. The team of developers is a team of vastly experienced teachers, with expertise in early childhood education, right-brain training, speech training, linguistics and music education. Our teachers are intensively trained to know the fundamentals of the programme and understand the developmental needs of children. The lessons are always conducted in an enjoyable manner so children can learn well in a positive environment.

Job Summary

Since 2008, Happy Train has enriched children's learning through a unique bilingual right brain training programme. As a teacher, you will deliver engaging lessons, monitor developmental progress, and foster positive relationships with children and parents, contributing to their holistic growth.

Responsibilities

  • Deliver lessons aligned with the Happy Train brain development curriculum, policies, and framework to facilitate children’s cognitive and language development
  • Prepare and organize teaching materials and learning aids to support experiential and multi-sensory learning activities
  • Monitor and evaluate children’s progress regularly to tailor instruction and provide feedback for continuous improvement
  • Establish and maintain positive rapport with children and parents to encourage a supportive learning environment and effective communication

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Demonstrate interpersonal, communication, and presentation skills to engage children and collaborate with parents effectively
  • Exhibit passion and commitment to nurturing young children’s development and learning attitudes
  • Possess proficiency in English to communicate clearly with children and parents, supporting bilingual programme delivery
  • Hold a Diploma or Degree in any major; fresh graduates are encouraged to apply
  • Availability to work a 5-day week from Wednesday to Sunday or a part-time 3-day week from Friday to Sunday, including weekends

Other Information

  • We provide specialised paid training to selected candidates to equip them with the skills needed for the programme
  • Attractive remuneration package with guaranteed bonus commensurate with experience and qualifications
  • Opportunities for career growth as we expand locally, including grooming teachers to manage their own centres
  • Interested applicants should send an email to enquiry@happytrain.com.sg with the header: Job Application
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be notified for interviews

raw_description:

Location: Near MRT stations of Telok Ayer, Downtown, Shenton Way and Raffles Place
Internship Type: Part-time Internship(≥ 3 days per week preferred)

Duration: 3–6 months (flexible, open to extension)
Working Arrangement: Onsite / Hybrid
Industry: Medical, with the parent company listed in China (Shanghai Stock Exchange A-share).

1. About the Role

We are looking for a detail-oriented and responsible intern to support our corporate and tax compliance team. This role is well-suited for university undergraduates who want hands-on exposure to Singapore government incentive programs and corporate administration work.

The intern will assist primarily with document organising, form preparation, and administrative support relating to EDB tax incentive programs and other regulatory filings, and other ad hoc tasks assigned by CEO.

2. Key Responsibilities

Ø Assist in organizing and maintaining corporate and tax-related documents (softcopy & hardcopy)

Ø Support the preparation and filling of application forms for EDB tax incentive programs and other government incentive-related filings, including liaison internally to collect required supporting documents (e.g. contracts, invoices,& other records)

Ø Help with data entry, reconciliation, and cross-checking of information in forms and schedules

Ø Assist in basic drafting of cover letters, checklists, and submission trackers

Ø Perform other general administrative and ad-hoc tasks assigned by CEO and other colleagues

3. Requirements

Ø Current undergraduate student from Singapore local universities, preferred majors: Accounting, Business, Finance, Economics, Law, Public Policy, or Operation

Ø Able to commit at least 3 months with immediate start

Ø Strong attention to detail and organised working style

Ø Proficient in Microsoft Office software (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).

Ø Good written and spoken English (Chinese is a plus but not mandatory)

Ø Responsible, discreet, and able to handle confidential information

Ø Good command of writing and speaking in both Mandarin and English is preferred.

title: SENIOR QUANTITY SURVEYOR
raw_description:
Manage and prepare all the cost estimation and contract administrative for the construction projects. This ranges from initial calculations to the final accounts. Your role will be highly critical in ensuring the financial success of our construction projects.

Responsibility:

  1. Reviews tender documents. Participation in the pre-tendering activities with client and project managers and raising relevant pre-bid queries from the given tender.
  2. Preparing VO. Provide detailed and accurate quantity and component take-offs against the drawings as specified in the tender document. Prepare project preliminary cost.
  3. Negotiating with suppliers and subcontractors to obtain the best prices and terms.
  4. Understanding the contract requirements, study and identify discrepancies in tender/contract documents and raise queries for clarifications.
  5. Managing project budgets, including forecasting and monitoring costs. Monitor and control costs throughout the project, identifying cost-saving opportunities.
  6. Check and verify the progress of work and submission of progress claims to client.
  7. Check and verify subcontracts/ vendors progress claims against the actual work and quality of work done and certify progress claim.
  8. Work closely with the Project Team to provide cost and contractual advice as well as to determine the valuation of work done at site.
  9. Maintaining accurate records and documentation of all project-related costs and financial transactions.

Required skills and qualifications：

  1. Tertiary Degree in Construction Management/Quantity Surveying.
  2. Sound knowledge of construction industry practices, regulations and standards.
  3. Proficiency in cost estimation and cost management techniques.
  4. Familiarity with contract administration and legal aspects of construction projects.
  5. Experience working with AutoCAD and validating quantities from the given drawings.
  6. Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and prioritise tasks effectively.
  7. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with extraordinary negotiation abilities.
  8. Proficiency in relevant software and tools for quantity surveying.
  9. Strong project management skills and ability to work under pressure.

raw_title: Algorithm Engineer Graduate (TikTok Shop Global E-Commerce, Supply Chain and Logistics) - 2026 Start (BS/MS)
title: Algorithm Engineer Graduate (TikTok Shop Global E-Commerce, Supply Chain and Logistics) - 2026 Start (BS/MS)
raw_description:
About TikTok

TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​

Why Join Us

Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.​

We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​

​

Diversity & Inclusion​

TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​

Job highlights

Yoga and fitness, Stock options, Positive team atmosphere, Career growth opportunity, Paid leave, Flat organization, 100+ mil users, Industry experts, Competitive compensation, Flexible hours

Responsibilities

Team Introduction

Our mission is to optimize the variety & cost-saving of assortments and the speed & quality of fulfillment to ensure the user experience of global e-commerce services.

- Build global logistic and warehousing network, improve operations efficiency and reduce operational cost with data analysis, machine learning and operation research methods.

- Create supply chain's data portrait and knowledge graph in various dimensions such as vendors, commodity, place of origin, inventory, production capacity and quality of fulfillment, etc. Predict user needs in different countries and regions, guide inventory preparation and fulfillment, and implement effective pricing. From a comprehensive B-C perspective, optimize platform efficiency and enhance user experience through diverse supply chain collaboration means.

- Optimize merchant/merchandise supply. Mine high-quality global merchant leads, establish outreach and conversion mechanism to drive merchant growth, and provide continuous incubation and support after merchant onboard. Track international e-commerce trends and TT content trends, identify best-selling product leads and improve the supply of high-quality and affordable goods. Collaborate with the supply chain system to build systematic product growth and operation capabilities.

We are looking for talented individuals to join our team in 2026. As a graduate, you will get opportunities to pursue bold ideas, tackle complex challenges, and unlock limitless growth. Launch your career where inspiration is infinite at TikTok.

Successful candidates must be able to commit to an onboarding date by end of year 2026. Please state your availability and graduation date clearly in your resume.

Candidates can apply to a maximum of two positions and will be considered for jobs in the order you apply. The application limit is applicable to TikTok and its affiliates' jobs globally. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis - we encourage you to apply early.

Responsibilities

- Responsible for the development of deep learning and operations research models and related intelligent systems for the supply chain and logistics of the global E-Commerce business.

- Utilize e-commerce big data and deep learning models to predict end-to-end estimated time of arrival (ETA), and some logistics events such as failed delivery, delivered but not received to enhance the user logistics experience. Build logistics network knowledge graphs and predict the spatio-temporal trajectory sequence of express packages through deep learning, statistical inference and other algorithmic methods. Use NLP and LLM algorithms to handle address problems such as address verification and address suggestion.

- Utilize time series forecasting techniques to predict sales at different granularities and horizons, such as warehouse-level manpower forecasting, inventory-level demand forecasting etc. We need strong machine learning and deep learning skills to detect important factors and model the relationship between the future and history.

- Participate in the development of merchant and merchanise related algorithm systems including potential merchants mining, tiering and outreach, seller growth diagnosis and seller mission recommendation system, etc. Work closely with e-commerce supply related operation teams and product teams to achieve the growth goal of e-commerce business.

- Based on the massive e-commerce related information mined from different resources, predict the traffic/conversion performance of merchandises using different kinds of machine learning methods, improve the efficiency of merchandise operation strategy and pricing/subsidy strategy, and assist in optimizing traffic distribution. And finally achieve the goal of merchandise growth under certain cost/profit target at the same time.

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications

- Final year graduates in Computer Science, engineering or quantitative field.

- Proficient in machine learning and statistics, familiar with the principles and model structures of common machine learning and deep learning algorithms for classification, regression, and clustering, with practical application experience.

- Proficient in programming languages such as Python/Java, possessing excellent coding abilities, and familiar with at least one common machine learning/deep learning framework (TensorFlow/PyTorch etc.).

Preferred Qualifications

- Demonstrates a spirit of exploration, outstanding data analysis capabilities, strong initiative, and excellent teamwork and communication skills.

- Bonus points for having published papers at conferences such as KDD, NeurIPS, WWW, SIGIR, WSDM, ICML, IJCAI, AAAI, RecSys, or having participated in data mining/machine learning competitions.

By submitting an application for this role, you accept and agree to our global applicant privacy policy, which may be accessed here: https://careers.tiktok.com/legal/privacy

If you have any questions, please reach out to us at apac-earlycareers@tiktok.com

title: Graduate Officer, Compliance
raw_description:
At Bank of Communications, we are dedicated to fostering talent and innovation in a dynamic and supportive environment, providing opportunities for growth and development. We are excited to invite enthusiastic and driven fresh graduates to apply for the position of Graduate Officer in our Compliance department. This is an entry-level role designed to provide on the job training and hands-on experience in the chosen field. You will work closely with seasoned professionals to gain valuable insights, contribute to meaningful projects and develop a strong foundation for your career.


· Report to Head of Compliance

· Assist in monitoring and ensuring adherence to company policies and regulatory requirements

· Support the development and implementation of compliance and AML program and initiatives

· Conduct research and analysis to identify potential compliance risks

· Collaborate with various departments to ensure compliance with internal and external standards

· Assist in AML/CFT name screening on a daily basis in a timely manner

· Coordinate with relevant departments/personnel to resolve any name screening issues including system issues

· Assist in the preparation of AML/CFT name screening statistics and reporting

· Assist in the preparation of training and guidance materials relating to name screening

· Recent graduates with a Bachelor’s degree from reputable Universities

· Strong interest in compliance and eagerness to learn

· Strong analytical skills, excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills

· Good communication skills both verbal and written with ability to interact comfortably with a variety of stakeholders.

title: Quality Assurance Engineer ( Junior )
raw_description:
About Us

Innovate. Create. Elevate.

At Vertis, we believe innovation starts with people. We design and deliver exceptional digital solutions that elevate our clients' brands and the experiences they create for their customers. Through technology-driven creativity, we enable meaningful business transformation. Our team is made up of capable, confident, and motivated individuals who collaborate, challenge ideas, and grow together.

About the Job

Join our dynamic team and play a crucial role in ensuring software quality. As a Junior QA Engineer, you will be introduced to the fundamentals of manual and automated testing, working under the guidance of experienced professionals to ensure product quality and performance.

Key Responsibilities

  • Learn and assist in the development, implementation, and maintenance of test scripts to ensure test coverage.
  • Collaborate with development and product teams to understand requirements and assist in designing test strategies.
  • Assist in identifying, documenting, and tracking defects, working closely with developers for resolution.
  • Perform basic functional and regression testing, with opportunities to learn automation tools.
  • Participate in continuous improvement of the testing process and framework.
  • Receive mentorship in conducting code reviews and understanding high-quality code standards.
  • Analyze test results with guidance and contribute to reports on product quality.
  • Engage in learning opportunities related to industry trends and advancements in testing tools and methodologies.
  • Assist in ensuring all deliverables meet quality standards and project timelines.

Requirements and Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
  • Basic understanding of software testing concepts and methodologies.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Excellent attention to detail and ability to follow instructions.
  • Good communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Ability to work effectively in a team environment.
  • Willingness to learn and grow in the field of software quality assurance.

Security Requirement (Mandatory)

This role involves work on Singapore Government projects. Candidates must have the legal right to work in Singapore and must be eligible for the required security clearance. Appointment to this role is subject to successful completion of all security vetting and clearance requirements.

Benefits

  • Best in class compensation
  • Medical Benefits
  • International and multicultural environment
  • Training & Development
  • Career opportunities in a successful, fast-growing company

Our Hiring Process

After submitting your application, our Talent Acquisition team will review your profile. If your skills and experience align with the role, we'll get in touch to discuss the next steps. Please be cautious of recruitment fraud; only engage with emails from our official team that end with @vertis.digital domain.

Vertis is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We believe that diversity of thought, background, and experience drives innovation and success. That's why we are committed to building and nurturing a team that reflects a wide range of perspectives, abilities, identities, and experiences. At Vertis, every voice matters, and together, we achieve more. We appreciate all applications; however, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Thank you for your interest.

The use of artificial intelligence tools, including language models such as ChatGPT, during the interview process is strictly prohibited and will result in the disqualification of your application.

Singapore Citizens are welcome to apply. This position is supported by IMDA's Company-Led Training Programme.

title: Geotechnical Design Engineer
raw_description:
We are looking for a Geotechnical Design Engineer with experience in geotechnical design, analysis and writing technical proposals. The Geotechnical Design Engineer should be knowledgeable in basic soil mechanics, as well as experienced in geotechnical analysis and design software.

Reporting to the Design Manager, you will have the opportunity to collaborate with the Business Development Team to provide design support for tenders. You will be expected to review tender specifications, requirements and draft the technical proposals. Likewise, you will be working closely with the Operations team to perform design-related coordination work, analysing geotechnical issues at project sites, and preparing detailed drawings for the site team.

To be successful as a Geotechnical Design Engineer, you should be independent, demonstrate strong analytical thinking and be able to have prompt, open and direct communications with internal and external stakeholders to achieve project expectations. Outstanding candidates should be meticulous, detail oriented, demonstrate willingness to learn and display a positive attitude with a drive for results.

Think you’ve got what it takes?

A day in the life of Geotechnical Design Engineer

Design support for tenders:

  • Review and summarize soil condition reports, tender specifications and requirements.
  • Identify and summarise geotechnical problems and technical risk.
  • Analyse geotechnical problems and draft technical proposal.

Design-related coordination work for ongoing projects:

  • Perform analysis and design in accordance with current legislation, standards and codes of practice
  • Prepare detailed construction drawings with the assistance of draughtsperson.
  • Conduct regular site visits to integrate design concept and actual ground conditions.
  • Quality Control (QC) review and verification between design assumptions and actual site conditions.
  • Carry out geotechnical analysis and design using tools such as Slope/W and Plaxis as well as Keller’s in-house software.
  • Conduct back analysis and research for future improvements based on actual performance and as-built data.
  • Prepare technical proposal for remedial work during construction when required.
  • Liaise with clients/consultants/ sub-contractors on technical related issues.

Note: This role requires candidates to perform regular site walks at outdoor construction sites

You will need:

  • A professional degree in Civil or Geotechnical Engineering
  • 1-3 years of relevant working experience in Geotechnical Engineering
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Powerpoint), AutoCAD, Plaxis 2D and Plaxis 3D
  • A valid Supervise Construction Work for WSH certificate (preferable)

What we offer:

  • Annual Leave, Medical Insurance, Medical Leave, Variable Bonus, Learning & Development Opportunities, Referral Rewards & Employee Assistance Programme
  • Global & Regional networking opportunities
  • Friendly team and enjoyable working environment

We encourage applications from the right candidates regardless of age, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, belief or race. Learn more about Keller's Inclusion Commitment at https://www.keller.com/how-we-work/diversity-equity-and-inclusion

Find out more here:

https://www.keller.com/

title: Retail Assistant
raw_description:
Are you ready to immerse yourself in the captivating world of scented products? Lynk Fragrances, a distinguished Singaporean brand, is seeking passionate individuals to join our team as Retail Consultants. As the leading scented product maker in the country, we are committed to creating exceptional fragrances that enchant the senses and elevate everyday moments. We take immense pride in our use of high-quality ingredients, combining them with artistry to produce exquisite products that our customers adore.

By joining us, you will be part of a pioneer team for our retail front in Changi Airport, Terminal 2. If you are a dedicated and enthusiastic individual with a deep appreciation for scented products, we invite you to embark on this scintillating journey with Lynk Artisan.

Basic Salary + Commission. Other Benefits include Medical insurance, Birthday Leave, etc.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Promote and educate customers about Lynk Fragrances .
  • Convert customers and close sales to meet or exceed sales targets.
  • Track daily sales and collect customer feedback to address any issues or concerns.
  • Provide accurate product information and answer customer inquiries.
  • Maintain a tidy and organized area to uphold the brand image.
  • Serve customers professionally and deliver excellent customer experiences.
  • Perform stock checks every morning.
  • Request replenishment of stocks for retail activities and/or campaigns.

Requirements:

  • Prior F2F sales experience and able to assist customers to achieve a satisfying and enjoyable shopping experience.
  • Strong communication skills and ability to engage customers effectively.
  • Positive mindset with a can-do attitude.
  • Professional appearance and customer-centric mindset.
  • Multi-lingual is a bonus.
  • Willing to work on weekends, including public holidays.
  • Fresh graduates with a positive attitude are welcome to apply!

raw_description:

Working Hours:
2 rotating shifts
• 8. 30 am to 5.00 pm
• 1.00 pm to 9.00 pm.

● Responsible to perform, depending on the section posted, a variety of test procedures according to the standards set in the Laboratory with little direct supervision by a senior staff
● Routine sample processing such as spinning and aliquoting of patient samples.

title: Category Support Executive
raw_description:
DESCRIPTION OF ROLE 

The Category Support Executive is responsible for providing end-to-end operational and administrative support to the Commercial team, ensuring the timely execution of category action plans, accurate system and master data maintenance, effective coordination with suppliers and internal stakeholders, and seamless delivery of promotions and commercial activities.

Key Responsibilities

  • Partner closely with the Category Manager to support assigned categories in achieving sales, profit, income, market share, and productivity targets.

  • Coordinate and execute category action plans, ensuring timely implementation to meet commercial objectives.

  • Provide end-to-end administrative support for new supplier onboarding and new product development (NPD) processes.

  • Ensure all cost, price, and commercial changes are accurately and promptly updated in the system.

  • Manage timely creation and maintenance of new articles and item master data.

  • Support the administration and monitoring of consignment-related activities.

  • Maintain accurate, timely promotional downloads and coordinate approvals for press advertisements.

  • Support weekly promotion commitments and work cross-functionally with Operations, Marketing, Supply Chain, and suppliers to ensure smooth execution.

  • Liaise with suppliers on administrative updates, documentation, and operational requirements.

  • Ensure timely and accurate submission of documents for system maintenance and master data updates.

  • Proactively support internal stakeholders and communicate potential risks or issues that may impact business operations.

  • Perform other ad-hoc duties as assigned to support the Category team.

About You

  • Diploma or Degree in any discipline.

  • Prior administrative or coordination experience/internship is preferred.

  • Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel (e.g. Pivot Tables, VLOOKUP).

  • Knowledge of SAP or similar ERP systems is an advantage.

  • Strong communication and coordination skills with the ability to work across multiple stakeholders.

  • Meticulous, detail-oriented, and highly organized, with the ability to manage multiple tasks concurrently.

title: Procurement Officer - WCAN
raw_description:
Responsibilities:

  • Oversee the full procurement lifecycle, including sourcing local and international suppliers, negotiating contracts, and issuing purchase orders.

  • Develop and implement procurement strategies to optimize costs and enhance operational efficiency.

  • Conduct market research to identify and evaluate potential suppliers based on quality, pricing, and reliability.

  • Manage company facilities, logistics, and shipping operations for both local and international activities.

  • Ensure compliance with company policies, legal regulations, ISO standards, and HSE requirements.

  • Administer all company licensing, insurance renewals, and vehicle maintenance and registration.

  • Resolve supplier disputes and address issues related to delivery, quality, or performance.

  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support project planning and procurement requirements.

  • Monitor inventory levels, maintain accurate records, and forecast future needs to ensure optimal stock.


Qualifications:

  • Diploma or Degree in Procurement, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Engineering, or a related discipline.

  • Minimum 3 years of practical procurement experience, preferably within M&E, construction, engineering, facilities management, or manufacturing industries.

  • Excellent negotiation, communication, and interpersonal skills.

  • Demonstrated ability to manage multiple procurement projects concurrently.

  • Detail-oriented with strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

  • Capable of working independently or collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.

  • Solid understanding of Singapore’s procurement regulations and industry best practices.

title: Graduate Trainee (Accounting Services)
raw_description:
Nexcore is a renowned consultancy firm dedicated to offering comprehensive solutions for businesses and individuals aiming for overseas operations and cross-border expansions. Our footprint spans globally, including pivotal regions like Singapore, Hong Kong, Japan, the US, Dubai, and China. Whether it's setting up company structures, accounting, or tax and legal compliance issues, our expert team is equipped to handle every challenge and ensure smooth business transitions.

Role Overview

As a Graduate Trainee (Accounting Services), you will receive hands-on training in bookkeeping and accounting support functions. This role is ideal for candidates who are looking to build a strong foundation in accounting and compliance, even without prior working experience.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist in bookkeeping and maintenance of accounting records
  • Support preparation of management accounts and financial schedules
  • Assist with bank reconciliations, invoices, and expense tracking
  • Prepare supporting documents for audit and tax filing purposes
  • Maintain proper documentation and filing of accounting records
  • Communicate with clients to follow up on accounting-related information
  • Support the accounting team in day-to-day operational tasks

Requirements

  • Diploma or Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, or related fields
  • Fresh graduates are welcome to apply; candidates with up to 1 year of accounting experience will be considered
  • Basic understanding of accounting principles is preferred (training will be provided)
  • Comfortable working with numbers and Excel
  • Good written and spoken English
  • Responsible, detail-oriented, and willing to learn

What We Offer

  • Structured training in accounting and compliance work
  • Exposure to SME and international client portfolios
  • Career development opportunities in professional services
  • Supportive team and learning environment
  • CPF contributions in accordance with local regulations

title: Mathematics Teacher
raw_description:
About Us:

Kangaroo Learning Centre Pte Ltd (“KLC”) was established in Singapore in May 2022 as part of the Fang Tian Education Group, a leading education institution founded in 2014 in China, which serves over 100,000 students annually.


We are one of Singapore’s fastest-growing math tuition centres. Out of 4000 students who participated in the Singapore Mathematical Olympiad (SMO), 118 students were invited for Rounds 2 and 51 are from our learning center. We are also the only centre in Singapore that offers parent sit-in during classes, a flexible refund policy, and a dual-teacher classroom model. Our unique approach and proven results have earned us a strong reputation among parents and students alike.


The centre offers a range of academic and enrichment programs for students of different ages, including primary and secondary school students, as well as those preparing for the PSLE, Math Olympiad, GCE ‘O’-Level and ‘A’-Level examinations.


At KLC, there's no office politics — we value direct, honest communication and a results-driven mindset. We’re not just looking for employees, but co-partners who are eager to grow with us, take initiative, and play an active role in building something meaningful together.


Job Overview:

At Kangaroo Learning Centre (KLC), our mission is to develop the best teachers in Singapore. We provide comprehensive training and a one-on-one mentorship program led by experienced senior teachers and management. Whether you're just starting out or looking to grow further, you'll receive the guidance, support, and structured development needed to thrive. Join us and build a rewarding teaching career with a clear path toward excellence, leadership, and long-term growth.


Job Responsibilities:

  • Create an interactive, inquiry-based, and student-centred classroom environment that guides and motivates students to enjoy learning mathematics.
  • Communicate closely with students, provide timely feedback to parents, and proactively support students in their learning.
  • Participate in center branding activities such as YouTube content creation, article writing and sharing, etc.
  • Familiarity with the question-setting patterns and trends of PSLE, O-Level, A-Level, and Math Olympiad competitions (e.g., RMO, APMOPS, NMOS, SMO) is an added advantage.
  • Support and participate in any teaching-related tasks and team activities assigned by the centre.

Job Requirements:

  • A bachelor’s degree or above from an accredited university (e.g., NUS, NTU, SMU, or equivalent).
  • Candidates without experience are welcome to apply.
  • Open to all nationalities (Sponsored for work passes)
  • No prior teaching experience required — comprehensive training and mentorship will be provided.
  • Responsible, adaptable, and willing to continuously learning.
  • Excellent interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills, with a positive, collaborative attitude and a strong passion for working closely with students, parents, and colleagues.
  • Able to commit weekdays (inc. evenings) & weekends (Note: Most student classes are held on weekends)

What We Offer

  • Performance-based salary reviews — high-performing teachers may receive salary of up to S$11,960 per month.
  • Additional S$1,000 monthly Guaranteed Teaching Allowance will be paid during the first 3 months probationary period.
  • The first year S$50,000 Guaranteed Annual Salary. We top up the balance if you didn’t get S$50,000 annual salary in the first year.
  • Structured one-on-one mentorship and coaching.
  • Comprehensive training — perfect for those starting their teaching career.
  • Dual career progression paths provided — grow into either a senior teaching expert or move towards leadership and management roles.
  • Opportunities to be involved in content creation, curriculum development, conducting activities with students and parents, and team leadership.

title: Research Assistant, School of Computing
raw_description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS Career Portal

Your application will be processed only if you apply via NUS Career Portal

We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Job Description

The National University of Singapore invites applications for the position of Research Fellow, Research Associate, or Research Assistant in the Department of Computer Science, School of Computing (SoC). SoC is strongly committed to research excellence in all its dimensions: Searching for fundamental results and insights, developing novel computational solutions to a wide range of applications, building large-scale experimental systems and improving the well-being of society. We seek to play an active role both internationally and locally in the core and emerging areas of Computer Science and Information Systems

The successful candidates will work closely with Principal Investigator Prof. Tulika Mitra on a recently funded Green AI project. This ambitious research initiative targets energy-efficient AI inference, co-optimizing across the full stack - from algorithms to architecture. The goal is to rethink data center computation and communication systems to make AI workloads significantly more sustainable. The ideal candidate will have experience in computing systems, such as ML systems, computer architecture, FPGA/GPU/accelerator design, compilers, or operating systems. We are looking for individuals who are passionate about systems-level innovation, eager to work across abstraction layers, and motivated to push the frontiers of sustainable and scalable AI

Only shortlisted candidates will be notified. 

Qualifications

•    PhD degree in a relevant area for research fellow and research associate position
•    Bachelors or Masters degree in a relevant area for research assistant position
•    Experience in research

title: Paralegal / Legal Executive
raw_description:
We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Paralegal / Legal Executive to join our Private Wealth Practice team. The successful candidate will assist our lawyers with various private wealth and asset management matters, including legal research, document preparation, and translation.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Assist in legal research, and document preparation.
  • Manage the document review process by coordinating comments from multiple sources and incorporating modifications under supervision.
  • Prepare preliminary and other basic deal documents.
  • Coordinate and manage document review processes by liaising with internal stakeholders, fund administrators, trustees, tax advisors and other professional parties, and consolidate comments and revisions.
  • Assist in cross-border matters involving Singapore, China and other jurisdictions, including translating legal documents and correspondence between English and Mandarin Chinese, where required.
  • Ensure confidentiality and adhere to ethical standards in all aspects of work.
  • Perform other duties as assigned.

Qualifications & Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Law or above; legal training and qualification preferred.
  • Minimum of two years of paralegal experience in a law firm or in-house legal department preferred.
  • Excellent organizational and communication skills.
  • Proficiency in English is required. Working proficiency in Mandarin is required to communicate with Mandarin-speaking stakeholders and review or translate basic Chinese-language materials.
  • Meticulous, able to multitask, and ensure transaction timelines are met.
  • Ownership-driven and proactive, able to manage matters independently and push projects forward.
  • Proficiency in MS Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

title: Clinical Associate
raw_description:
The National University Health System (NUHS) is an integrated Academic Health System and Regional Health System in Singapore that delivers value-driven, innovative and sustainable healthcare. Institutions in the NUHS group include three hospitals – National University Hospital, Ng Teng Fong General Hospital and Jurong Community Hospital; three National Specialty Centres – National University Cancer Institute, Singapore, National University Heart Centre, Singapore and National University Centre for Oral Health, Singapore; a polyclinic group – the National University Polyclinics; one medical centre – Jurong Medical Centre; and three academic health sciences institutions – National University of Singapore (NUS) Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine (including the Alice Lee Centre for Nursing Studies), NUS Faculty of Dentistry and NUS Saw Swee Hock School of Public Health.


The Departments are seeking candidates who are highly motivated and willing to join us for a challenging and fulfilling appointment as Clinical Associate on a contractual basis.


As a clinical associate, you will be responsible for the total management of patients in the ward and administrative duties required of the department to provide direct patient care. In addition, you will be responsible for assisting the Registrar and Senior Staff in the running of the Specialist Outpatient Clinics.

•Be in active clinical practice •Possess a basic medical degree registrable with the Singapore Medical Council

•Possess a 3 year of post housemanship clinical experience

•Must be registered with the Singapore Medical Council

•Able to work independently with demonstrated competence in managing patients in the department

•Confident, takes initiatives and a good team player

title: Business Analyst ( Junior )
raw_description:
About Us

Innovate. Create. Elevate.

At Vertis, we believe innovation starts with people. We design and deliver exceptional digital solutions that elevate our clients' brands and the experiences they create for their customers. Through technology-driven creativity, we enable meaningful business transformation. Our team is made up of capable, confident, and motivated individuals who collaborate, challenge ideas, and grow together.

About the job

We are looking for a passionate and detail-oriented Junior Business Analyst to join our dynamic team. In this role, you will assist in gathering and analyzing business requirements, supporting documentation efforts, and facilitating communication between business stakeholders and technical teams. This is a fantastic opportunity for someone eager to develop their skills in business analysis, digital transformation, and agile methodologies.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist in gathering, documenting, and analyzing business and system requirements.
  • Support requirements discussions and workshops with clients and internal teams.
  • Collaborate with developers, testers, and project managers to ensure the solution meets business needs.
  • Help translate business requirements into clear, concise functional specifications and user stories.
  • Participate in system testing, including preparation of test cases and test execution.
  • Assist in the preparation of user manuals, training materials, and process documentation.
  • Contribute to post-implementation reviews and user feedback sessions.
  • Support project teams in the implementation of Agile or SDLC processes.
  • Maintain and update documentation in tools such as JIRA and Confluence.
  • Participate in research, analysis, and proposal writing for business solutions.

Requirements and Qualification

  • 1–3 years of experience in a Business Analyst or similar role. Fresh graduates are encouraged to apply.
  • A Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field.
  • Basic understanding of the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) and Agile methodology.
  • Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving abilities.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
  • Familiarity with JIRA, Confluence, or similar tools is a plus.
  • Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident (SPR).

Security Requirement (Mandatory)

This role involves work on Singapore Government projects. Candidates must have the legal right to work in Singapore and must be eligible for the required security clearance. Appointment to this role is subject to successful completion of all security vetting and clearance requirements.

Benefits

  • Competitive pay
  • Medical benefits
  • International and multicultural work environment
  • Continuous training and development opportunities
  • Birthday leave to celebrate your special day
  • Career growth in a dynamic, fast-growing company

Our Hiring Process
After submitting your application, our Talent Acquisition team will review your profile. If your skills and experience align with the role, we'll get in touch to discuss the next steps. Please be cautious of recruitment fraud, only engage with emails from our official that end with @vertis.digital domain.

Vertis is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We believe that diversity of thought, background, and experience drives innovation and success. That's why were committed to building and nurturing a team that reflects a wide range of perspectives, abilities, identities, and experiences. At Vertis, every voice matters, and together, we achieve more. We appreciate all applications; however, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Thank you for your interest.

The use of artificial intelligence tools, including language models such as ChatGPT, during the interview process is strictly prohibited and will result in the disqualification of your application.

Singapore Citizens are welcome to apply. This position is supported by IMDAs Company-Led Training Programme.
