title: L1 IT Support Specialist (Retail & Office)
raw_description:
Responsibilities

  • Provide first-line IT support for corporate office and boutique users, ensuring smooth daily operations across retail and business environments.
  • Troubleshoot and resolve hardware, software, network, and connectivity issues for end-user devices such as laptops, printers, mobile devices, and peripherals.
  • Perform user and access administration, including email, Office 365 accounts, and system permissions, in line with IT policies.
  • Support device lifecycle activities such as setup, deployment, replacement, and decommissioning for staff movements and store openings.
  • Assist with routine IT operations including system updates, patching, basic maintenance, and health monitoring under guidance from senior IT members.
  • Maintain accurate IT asset records, manage software installations and licences, and coordinate with vendors for repairs, replacements, and warranties.
  • Support basic network and infrastructure matters (LAN, Wi-Fi, VPN), and assist during boutique renovations, relocations, or IT upgrades.
  • Log, track, and document incidents and resolutions in the ticketing system, support security and compliance requirements, and provide operational support for IT projects and go-live activities.

Requirements:

  • Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related discipline, with 2–5 years of hands-on IT support experience.
  • Experience supporting end users in office and/or retail environments, with working knowledge of Windows/macOS, Office 365, and basic networking.
  • Familiarity with IT service desk tools, asset management, hardware/software troubleshooting, and basic IT security practices; POS system exposure is an advantage.
  • Customer-focused, organised, and dependable, with strong communication skills and the flexibility to support a fast-paced retail environment, including occasional after-hours support when required.

title: AI Research Scientist
raw_description:
About Bitdeer:

Bitdeer Technologies Group (Nasdaq: BTDR) is a leader in the blockchain and high-performance computing industry. It is one of the world’s largest holders of proprietary hash rate and suppliers of hash rate. Bitdeer is committed to providing comprehensive computing solutions for its customers.

Headquartered in Singapore, Bitdeer has deployed mining data centers in the United States, Norway, and Bhutan. It offers specialized mining infrastructure, high-quality hash rate sharing products, and reliable hosting services to global users. The company also offers advanced cloud capabilities for customers with high demands for artificial intelligence.

About Bitdeer AI Lab:

Bitdeer AI Lab is a frontier AI Lab under Bitdeer, a global-leading computing power solutions provider. We will focus on two core missions: First, effectively transforming energy into intelligence; second, pushing the limits of intelligence by redefining AI architectures to achieve human-level learning efficiency and reasoning capabilities. Our vision is to create intelligence that learns like humans do: efficiently, adaptively, and recursively, transforming finite parameters into unbounded potential.

Our lab is equipped with thousands of cutting-edge GPUs dedicated to AI research, and we are committed to continuously investing in and expanding our computational infrastructure to support research in artificial intelligence.

Job Summary:

We are looking for key talents to develop in the following areas

  • LLM Area (Large-scale language model pre-training)
  • Reinforcement learning for reasoning models
  • Advanced AI systems (Data construction, curation, and engineering for large-scale model training )

Our ideal candidate:

  • Fresh graduates (Bachelors, Masters, PhD) from all disciplines and/or candidates with up to 2 years of related work experience.
  • Strong background in the LLM era with hands-on experience in: Large-scale language model pre-training (preferably 3B+ parameter models with 500B+ token datasets)
  • Reinforcement learning for reasoning models and advanced AI systems - Data construction, curation, and engineering for large-scale model training
  • Understanding of distributed training, scaling laws, and optimization techniques （I）Deep passion for cutting-edge AI technologies and research （II）Impressive personal achievements in academics, research projects, internships, or demonstrated expertise in AI/ML domains
  • Most importantly: driven to create meaningful impact early in your career, with strong ownership mentality, accountability, and unwavering commitment to pursuing excellence in AI research

What you will experience working with us:

  • A culture that values authenticity and diversity of thoughts and backgrounds;
  • An inclusive and respectable environment with open workspaces and exciting start-up spirit;
  • Fast-growing company with the chance to network with industrial pioneers and enthusiasts;
  • Ability to contribute directly and make an impact on the future of the digital asset industry;
  • Involvement in new projects, developing processes/systems;
  • Personal accountability, autonomy, fast growth, and learning opportunities;
  • Attractive welfare benefits and developmental opportunities such as training and mentoring.

title: Embedded Software Engineer (Aerospace)
raw_description:
Job Description:

  • In charge of designing the PCB layout of electronic circuit boards and generate Gerber files for PCB fabrication.
  • Responsible in research and define efficient and reliable architecture to fulfil electronic circuit board requirements for the company drone systems.
  • In charge of firmware programming in embedded C/C++ language.
  • In charge of testing PCB assemblies thoroughly and ensure they operate as expected.
  • Responsible in sourcing suitable electronic components to implement the design architecture.
  • In charge of studying datasheets of the electronic components and design the schematic of electronic circuit boards that can fulfil the requirements.
  • Responsible in generateing documents and drawings of design and test.

Job Requirement:

  • Minimum Degree in Mechanical/ Electrical/ Aerospace/ Mechatronics Engineering or relevant
  • Minimum 3 years of working experience in electronic hardware and firmware design
  • Experience in analog and digital circuit design with experience in designing electronic circuits with microcontrollers, digital signal processors (DSP), field programmable gate arrays (FPGAs), etc
  • Proficient in schematic and PCB design tools such as Altium Designer, OrCAD Capture and PCB Designer, Autodesk EAGLE, etc and using instruments such as multimeters, oscilloscopes, logic analysers, etc. to troubleshoot issues on electronic circuit boards.
  • Proficient in C/C++ programming language.
  • Knowledge in various communication buses and protocols such as UART, I2C, SPI, CAN, Ethernet, etc.

raw_description:
ABOUT GLOBAL BANKING & MARKETS
Our core value is building strong relationships with our institutional clients, which include corporations, financial service providers, and fund managers. We help them buy and sell financial products on exchanges around the world, raise funding, and manage risk. This is a dynamic, entrepreneurial team with a passion for the markets, with individuals who thrive in fast-paced, changing environments and are energized by a bustling trading floor.

Global Banking & Markets: Transformation Office 
Are you a looking for a new opportunity to showcase your intellectual curiosity, advisory and project management skills? Do you have a strategic mindset and the ability to influence change? Do you wish to be valued for your innovative approach to problem-solving? We have an excellent new position available within our Transformation Office team for Global banking & Markets.

The Global Banking & Markets Transformation Office Team is searching for dedicated individuals who thrives in a collaborative and challenging environment to join our team. Global Change Managers serve as program or product managers on high priority initiatives within the Global Banking & Markets Division. You will work alongside other Change Managers, Product Managers, Sales, Strats, Engineers and Designers to ensure solutions are designed and implemented for the Global Banking & Markets Division in true ‘front to back’ fashion. You will leverage your technical expertise and project management skills to influence and drive the team to deliver these complex projects within budget and with a high quality. 

HOW YOU WILL FULFILL YOUR POTENTIAL

  • Work with Sales, Trading, Engineering and Operations teams to develop and implement strategic complex initiatives to enable business growth, regulatory compliance and control improvements 
  • Leverage project management methodologies (e.g. Waterfall, Agile) and governance tools to provide transparency to senior stakeholders on project scope, timelines, budget and delivery risks 
  • Identify and manage project risks from inception to completion while driving resolutions and risk & issue mitigants 
  • Perform detailed analysis to enhance business, system & operational workflows
  • Facilitate communication on key project matters (incl. reporting and escalations) across GBM teams, including Sales, Operations, Engineering, Legal, Compliance
  • Provide oversight on the project book of work, ensuring cross project dependencies, risks, and budget are proactively managed 
  • Facilitate the performance of quality assurance testing, migration plans and post implementation reviews alongside product

SKILLS & EXPERIENCE WE’RE LOOKING FOR

  • Bachelor's Degree
  • 1-3 years’ relevant experience, preferably in a business analysis / project / program management function
  • Strong analytical and business analysis experience, including process analysis 
  • Strategic thinker able to identify cross functional issues, provide innovative solutions, and communicate the implications of solutions across existing processes, systems and teams, e.g. costs, risk, service
  • Project management skills including project scope definition; project issues, challenges, risks definition; status reporting; User Acceptance Test execution
  • A proven track record of scoping and driving delivery of projects. Has delivered results in a cross functional team setting, on time and to budget
  • Experienced in driving collaboration and facilitate discussions with stakeholders to perform problem solving and solution analysis 
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, written and oral; confident in interaction with senior stakeholders

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

  • Knowledge of products and processes related to the financial services industry would be an advantage, as is related experience in technology strategy or in a client service or business function and / or management consulting
  • Core knowledge base of Global Banking & Markets
  • Demonstrate understanding of Financial Services & Operations processes and procedures 
  • Familiarity with project management methodologies and concepts, Project Management related certifications preferred but optional (e.g. PMP, Prince2, Agile, etc.)
  • Experience developing products using customer and design-centric practices
  • Knowledge of automated testing tools and frameworks, Agile Testing practices
  • Experience on tools like JIRA and JIRA Portfolio or similar applications
  • Knowledge of OKR frameworks and roadmaps, as well as other governance and project planning tooling 

ABOUT GOLDMAN SACHS
At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world. 

We believe who you are makes you better at what you do. We're committed to fostering and advancing diversity and inclusion in our own workplace and beyond by ensuring every individual within our firm has a number of opportunities to grow professionally and personally, from our training and development opportunities and firmwide networks to benefits, wellness and personal finance offerings and mindfulness programs. Learn more about our culture, benefits, and people at GS.com/careers. 

We’re committed to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates with special needs or disabilities during our recruiting process. Learn more: https://www.goldmansachs.com/careers/footer/disability-statement.html

title: Research Fellow / Engineer (Food Engineering) - AZN5
raw_description:
As a University of Applied Learning, SIT works closely with industry in our research pursuits. Our research staff will have the opportunity to be equipped with applied research skill sets that are relevant to industry demands while working on research projects in SIT. 

The primary purpose of this role is to deliver on a collaborative research project, Proteins for Singapore (P4SG), where you will be part of the research team to establish and understand the structuration of algae/soybean/mung bean proteins for high quality alternative protein foods including through high moisture extrusion.

Key responsibilities will include:

  • Explore innovative methods for food process optimization including the use of AI and machine-learning
  • Develop and execute methods for characterizing and linking the texture and structure of alternative protein foods
  • Design, execute, and interpret experiments independently.
  • Participate in and manage the research project with Principal Investigators (PI), and the research team members to ensure all project deliverables including periodic progress reports are met.
  • Author papers in high-quality journals and contribute to grant writing in areas that are related to the research.
  • Carry out Risk Assessment, and ensure compliance with Work, Safety and Health Regulations.
  • Coordinate procurement and liaison with vendors/suppliers.
  • Work independently, as well as manage a team of junior researchers and students to ensure proper operation and maintenance of equipment.
  • To communicate and liaise with any internal and external stakeholders to ensure project deliverables are met.
  • Any other ad-hoc duties assigned by supervisor.

Ideal qualities of the candidate include:

  • Able to build and maintain strong working relationships with people within and external to the university.
  • Self-directed learner who believes in continuous learning and development
  • Highly proficient in technical writing and presentation 
  • Strong analytical and critical thinking skills
  • Strong initiative and takes ownership of work

The candidate should have relevant competence in the areas of processing technology, physical and structural analysis, material formulation, and mathmatical modeling. The candidate should also have a PhD or other advanced degree in a food or engineering related field. Knowledge of food formulation, sensory analysis, extrusion technology, machine learning, and rheology will be advantageous.

raw_description:
Our client is one of the local leading Herbal & Wellness Beverage Companies. Due to business needs, they are now recruiting a Marketing Executive to be part of their Marketing team for ongoing transformation projects. They are located in the North – easily accessible.

  • Support customer engagement, CRM activities, and lead management.

Qualifications:

  • Singaporean / Singapore PR preferred
  • Minimum 21 years old with Polytechnic Diploma, GCE 'A' level Certification or Bachelor Degree in any field, as per MAS requirements.
  • Highly motivated with strong interpersonal skills

title: Office Assistant
raw_description:
Job Overview

As an Office Assistant, you will provide general administrative and clerical support to ensure smooth day-to-day office operations. This role involves assisting with office coordination, documentation, basic data entry, and supporting staff and management, including handling all information with strict confidentialityand professionalism. To succeed in this role, you should be dependable, organised, detail-oriented and able to manage multiple tasks efficiently in a professional office environment.

Responsibilities

Administrative & Office Support

  • Assist in organising and maintaining office common areas to ensure a clean and orderly environment.
  • Manage day-to-day office operations and administrative tasks
  • Maintain office supplies,equipment, and facilities
  • Handle incoming calls, emails, mail, and courier arrangements
  • Assist with data entry, basic bookkeeping, invoice processing, claims and expense record management as required.

Scheduling & Coordination

  • Organise and support meetings,events, and conferences, including logistics and arrangements.

Documentation & Records Management

  • Prepare, file, and maintain documents, reports, and correspondence
  • Maintain accurate records, databases and filing systems (both physical and digital).
  • Assist in preparing presentations and basic reports

General Support

  • Provide administrative support to management and team members.
  • Confident in serving guests during lunch and dinner service when required.
  • Handle ad hoc personal tasks for Heads of Departments as required.
  • Any other duties as assigned


Requirements

  • Minimum Diploma/ Degree in Business Administration or related field
  • 2-4 years of relevant office administration experience
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)
  • Prior experience or industry knowledge in Accounting and/or F&B will be an advantage
  • Ability to manage multiple tasks and prioritise competing demands to meet deadlines
  • A professional and approachable demeanour with strong verbal and written communication skills.
  • Strong organisational and time-management skills with attention to detail.
  • Responsible and reliable, with the ability to handle all information confidentially and professionally

title: Research Fellow (Cellular Agriculture)
raw_description:
The School of Materials Science and Engineering (MSE) provides a vibrant and nurturing environment for staff and students to carry out inter-disciplinary research in key areas such as Computational Materials Science, Characterisation Materials Science, Defence Composite Materials, Functional Composite Materials, Energy, Nanomaterials, Low Dimensional Materials, Biomaterials Materials, Biological Materials, Bioinspired Materials and Sustainable Materials.

For more details, please view https://www.ntu.edu.sg/mse/research.

The antimicrobial peptide chemistry group, led by Professor Zvi Hayouka, has world-leading expertise in multiple research areas, such as peptide chemistry, peptide modifications, microbiology, antimicrobial peptides design and synthesis, solid phase chemistry, peptide bacterial binders, and biosensing. We are looking to hire a Research Fellow. The candidate should adapt to new peptide synthesis and discovery techniques and support the surface assembly processes of the lead peptides, antimicrobial assays development, and electrochemical studies efforts led by the other partners in the consortia. The candidate shall work under the supervision of the Principal Investigator, who will be most of the time via remote supervision, to conduct research work as follows:

Key Responsibilities:

  • Collects and prepare data/ information/ proposal required for relevant grant call process
  • Conducts experiments related to the synthesis and characterization of peptide bacterial binders or antimicrobial peptides
  • Assists in interpreting and applying processes and procedures to conduct/monitor project progress
  • Gathers information/ data and prepare documents for reporting/ papers
  • Assists in and delivers training and coaching new members with relevant laboratory/ technical skills
  • Report remotely to the PI and travel to Israel for training

Job Requirements:

  • Ph.D. degree in Peptide Chemistry/Microbiology
  • Strong experimental skills, especially in peptide chemistry and mainly in the design of antimicrobial peptides and characterize their antimicrobial and selectivity profile.
  • Excellent communication and organizational skills while hard-working and able to meet tight deadlines
  • Able to work independently in gathering information/ data and preparing documents for reporting/ papers
  • Willing to contribute to team services like sharing/ coaching new members with relevant laboratory/ technical skills

We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Research Engineer (Temporary Bonding and Debonding) (APM), IME
raw_description:
As a Research Engineer in the Wafer-to-Wafer (W2W) Bonding Module, you will work on Temporary Bonding and Debonding (TBDB) processes—an essential step in advanced semiconductor packaging for handling ultra-thin wafers. This role focuses on process development, optimization, and stabilization to enable next-generation packaging technologies.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Developing and optimizing temporary bonding and debonding processes for wafer-level packaging.
  • Support integration of process flows, ensuring compatibility across multiple steps in advanced packaging including laser debonding and back grinding.
  • Investigate and resolve process issues and defects such as voids, warpage, and delamination.
  • Plan and execute Design of Experiments (DOE) for process development.
  • Collect, analyze data, and document findings in technical reports.

Job Requirements:

  • Bachelor's degree in Materials Science, Mechanical Engineering, Physics, Chemistry, Electronics, or Electrical Engineering.
  • Experience in TBDB process development or advanced packaging technologies is an advantage.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
  • Good communication and teamwork skills.
  • Ability to work in a cleanroom environment and handle precision equipment.

title: Electronics Engineer
raw_description:
A Electronics Engineer supports engineering activities such as design, test, verification, modification, fabrication and assembly of prototype electromechanical systems, experimental design circuitry or specialized test equipment.

Responsibilities:

  • Demonstrated ability to independently work from electrical schematics, diagrams, written and verbal descriptions, layouts or defined plans to perform testing, checkout, and trouble-shooting functions.
  • Assemble and test electrical components. This includes mechanical setup of housings, chassis, etc.
  • Work safely with high voltage components in excess of 100V with large energy storage.
  • Demonstrated history of assembling electrical components – PCBA’s, capacitors, FETs, diodes, surface mount, through-hole parts, inductors, etc.
  • Demonstrated soldering skills of small and large electrical components.
  • Operate electrical test equipment to analyze electrical circuits, troubleshoot and measure V, I, impedance, frequency response, etc. Demonstrated history of operation of oscilloscopes, spectrum analyzers, network analyzers, LCR meters in a R&D, non-production-test environment.
  • Knowledge of the function of capacitors, resistors, LRC circuits.
  • Performs operational test and fault isolation on systems and equipment. Assists in determining methods or actions to remedy malfunctions.  Find failed or damaged components within PCBA’s, trace signals to determine electrical circuit issues.
  • Support engineering in writing ECO’s, organizing BOM’s, work orders. Understanding of assembly processes.
  • Occasional documentation of build procedures and tests of electrical components.
  • Document tests, take data, analyze data to determine next actions.
  • Work with electrical component vendors, acquire parts, understanding of PCBA assembly


Experience & Skillsets:

  • Bachelor of Electrical Engineering
  • Has knowledge of basic electrical circuits and common practices in electrical assembly.
  • Must have strong electro-mechanical problem-solving attributes.
  • Strong skills in operation of electrical test equipment – oscilloscopes, network analyzers, spectrum analyzers, volt meters.
  • Manual soldering skills, includes fine pitch components.
  • Good verbal skills to understand instructions, report results
description: A Electronics Engineer supports engineering activities such as design, test, verification, modification, fabrication and assembly of prototype electromechanical systems, experimental design circuitry or specialized test equipment. title: Talent Acquisition Executive
raw_description:
Fresh Graduates Are Welcome to Apply

Key Responsibilities

Recruitment and Placement

  • Manage the end-to-end recruitment process for frontline positions, including job postings, resume screening, phone interviews, interview coordination, and offer communication.
  • Work closely with business stakeholders to clearly understand job responsibilities and hiring requirements for frontline roles.
  • Develop, maintain, and expand recruitment channels for blue-collar hiring.
  • Effectively utilize both online and offline recruitment platforms, including but not limited to LinkedIn, JobStreet, MyCareersFuture, and on-site job fairs.
  • Establish and manage employee referral programs to encourage internal talent recommendations.
  • Leverage social media platforms (e.g. WeChat, TikTok) to enhance employer branding and promote job opportunities.

Candidate Relationship Management

  • Build strong, professional relationships with candidates and provide a positive, responsive candidate experience.
  • Support offer discussions, coordinate onboarding schedules, and assist with background checks.
  • Track onboarding progress and liaise with hiring managers and administrative teams to ensure smooth employee integration.

Data and Process Optimization

  • Track and analyze recruitment metrics such as interview attendance, offer acceptance rates, and turnover data.
  • Prepare regular recruitment reports and continuously improve recruitment processes to enhance efficiency, quality, and cost control.

Qualifications & Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree or above in Human Resource Management, Psychology, Business Administration, or related disciplines.
  • 1–2 years of relevant recruitment experience preferred; fresh graduates with strong interest in recruitment are welcome to apply.
  • Familiarity with blue-collar recruitment channels and hiring practices in industries such as manufacturing, logistics, or labor-intensive sectors is an advantage.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage frontline employees from diverse backgrounds.
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple priorities effectively.
  • Proactive, responsible, and results-driven mindset.

raw_title: Natural Language Processing Research Fellow (Thai Language Specialist) for AI Singapore (Products)
title: Natural Language Processing Research Fellow (Thai Language Specialist) for AI Singapore (Products)
raw_description:
AI Singapore (AISG) is a national AI programme launched by the National Research Foundation (NRF) to anchor deep national capabilities in Artificial Intelligence (AI).

The programme office is hosted by the Nanyang Technological University and brings together all Singapore-based research institutions and the vibrant ecosystem of AI start-ups and companies developing AI products to perform use-inspired research, grow the knowledge, create the tools, and develop the talent to power Singapore's AI efforts.

We are seeking a highly motivated and talented Research Fellow to join our team and contribute to cutting-edge research in multilingual Large Language Models (LLMs) and Natural Language Processing (NLP), with a specific focus on Southeast Asian languages. This is an exciting opportunity to work in a dynamic and rapidly evolving field, pushing the boundaries of language technology and making a real impact on under-represented languages.

Key Responsibilities:

• Lead research initiatives in pre-training,post-training, alignment, or evaluation

• Define research direction and roadmap within the Hub team

• Design and execute experiments; establish rigorous evaluation frameworks

• Mentor AI Researchers; guide experiment design and research methodology Translate research findings into production-ready improvements for SEA-LION models

• Publish papers, present at conferences, and represent AISG in the research community

• Collaborate with Engineering to ensure research outputs are deployable

• May lead smaller research teams or cross-functional initiatives Accountable for: Research leadership and advancing model capabilities

Job Requirements:

• PhD in Computer Science, Linguistics, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field, with a focus on NLP or machine learning.

• Strong publication track record in top-tier NLP conferences (e.g., ACL, EMNLP, NAACL) and journals (e.g., TACL,JAIR).

• Demonstrated experience in conducting NLP research involving Southeast Asian languages.

• Native fluency in at least one major Southeast Asian language (e.g., Indonesian, Malay, Thai, Vietnamese, Filipino/Tagalog). Fluency in multiple Southeast Asian languages is highly preferred.

• Strong hands-on experience with PyTorch and Hugging Face Transformers library.

• Proven ability to train and fine-tune large language models using GPUs.

• Strong understanding of machine learning and deep learning concepts.

• Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

• Comfortable operating in a rapidly evolving field of research.

• Highly motivated and passionate about NLP and multilingual research.

• Excellent team player with strong interpersonal skills.

• Ability toadapt to changing priorities and deadlines.

• Strong publication record and demonstrated research impact

• Deep expertise in LLM training, alignment, or evaluation

• Ability to balance research exploration with practical delivery

Bonus if you have:

• Knowledge of computational linguistics and phonetics.

• Experience with cloud based computing platforms.

We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Clinical Associate
raw_description:
The National University Health System (NUHS) is an integrated Academic Health System and Regional Health System in Singapore that delivers value-driven, innovative and sustainable healthcare. Institutions in the NUHS group include three hospitals – National University Hospital, Ng Teng Fong General Hospital and Jurong Community Hospital; three National Specialty Centres – National University Cancer Institute, Singapore, National University Heart Centre, Singapore and National University Centre for Oral Health, Singapore; a polyclinic group – the National University Polyclinics; one medical centre – Jurong Medical Centre; and three academic health sciences institutions – National University of Singapore (NUS) Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine (including the Alice Lee Centre for Nursing Studies), NUS Faculty of Dentistry and NUS Saw Swee Hock School of Public Health.


The Departments are seeking candidates who are highly motivated and willing to join us for a challenging and fulfilling appointment as Clinical Associate on a contractual basis.


As a clinical associate, you will be responsible for the total management of patients in the ward and administrative duties required of the department to provide direct patient care. In addition, you will be responsible for assisting the Registrar and Senior Staff in the running of the Specialist Outpatient Clinics.

  • Be in active clinical practice
  • Possess a basic medical degree registrable with the Singapore Medical Council
  • Possess a 3 year of post housemanship clinical experience
  • Must be registered with the Singapore Medical Council
  • Able to work independently with demonstrated competence in managing patients in the department
  • Confident, takes initiatives and a good team player

title: Product Development Engineer
raw_description:
THE ROLE: 

As a Product Development engineer, you will collaborate with various Product Development Groups to bring-up and ramp new products which include Plan, debug and handling manufacturing and characterization test programs for next-generation AMD microprocessors. 

THE PERSON: 

A successful candidate is one who can perform in a role that requires accountability for accuracy, reliability and completeness of assignment results critical to the project’s success.  

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 

  • Validate new product features, test conditions, test methodologies, and test contents during first silicon bring-up 
  • Post Silicon Bring up of Test patterns leading to optimization for mass production enablement 
  • Analysis of part failures leading to test improvement 
  • Test pattern & test flow development, debug, test and characterization 

PREFERRED EXPERIENCE: 

  • Knowledge of scripting languages such as Perl, Ruby, shell script 
  • Good understanding of Linux, Unix or Windows OS 
  • ATE (Automatic Test Equipment) test experience 

ACADEMIC CREDENTIALS:

title: Junior Finance & Trade Admin Executive
raw_description:
Role Overview

We are a Singapore-based commodities trading group seeking a Junior Finance & Trade Admin Executive to support our finance and trading operations.

This role focuses on trade finance documentation, banking administration, and finance support, with secondary responsibility for general office administration and coordination. It is a hands-on support role suited for a detail-oriented individual who is comfortable working with documentation, banks, and internal stakeholders in a fast-paced trading environment.

Key Responsibilities

Trade Finance & Trade Administration (Primary)

  • Prepare, compile, and submit KYC / CDD documentation to banks and trading counterparties

  • Handle trade documentation including Bills of Lading (BL), invoices, packing lists, certificates, and other shipping documents

  • Coordinate with banks on:

    • Trade finance documentation (e.g. LC, trust receipts, scan BL submissions)

    • Facility utilisation requests and document follow-ups

  • Maintain well-organised and audit-ready trade documentation records

  • Track trade documentation from pre-shipment to post-shipment stages

Finance Support

  • Assist with basic bookkeeping support, reconciliations, and invoice processing

  • Support payment and collection tracking

  • Maintain simple cashflow and trade tracking spreadsheets

  • Provide administrative support to the finance team as required

Office Administration (Secondary)

  • Coordinate office service providers (cleaning, pantry, stationery, IT vendors)

  • Manage office supplies and general filing

  • Support basic HR administration such as leave tracking and onboarding documentation

  • Ensure smooth day-to-day office operations

Requirements

  • Diploma or Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, Logistics, or a related field

  • 1–3 years of relevant working experience, preferably in:

    • Commodity trading or trading support roles

    • Trade finance or banking operations

    • Shipping, logistics, or documentation-focused roles

  • Familiarity with trade documentation and banking processes is an advantage

  • Proficient in Microsoft Excel and comfortable working with documentation

  • Strong attention to detail and good organisational skills

  • Able to work independently and manage multiple tasks

  • Comfortable in a support role with a mix of finance and administrative responsibilities

Salary & Benefits

  • Monthly Salary Range: SGD 3,200 – 4,000

  • Salary commensurate with experience and skills

  • Stable role with exposure to commodities trading and trade finance operations

Reporting Line

  • Reports directly to the Head of Finance

title: Legal Associate
raw_description:
About the Role

We are expanding our Singapore legal team and are seeking a Legal Associate to support a group of Singapore-qualified lawyers advising on sophisticated and cross-border financial services and FinTech matters. This role offers hands-on exposure to FinTech advisory, licensing, and regulatory work, including matters relating to derivatives broking, payments and financial markets activities. The Singapore team is part of a broader regional and global group of legal and compliance professionals.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support Legal Counsels on a wide range of FinTech, payments and financial services legal matters.
  • Assist with licensing, regulatory advisory work, and contractual matters across multiple jurisdictions.
  • Work on legal and regulatory issues relating to derivatives broking, payments and financial markets.
  • Draft, review, and analyse legal documentation, policies, and regulatory submissions.
  • Conduct legal research and provide practical, solution-oriented support.
  • Work collaboratively with legal, compliance, and business teams as part of a wider function.

What We're Looking For

  • Bachelor's degree in Law from Singapore or other jurisdictions; bar qualification is a bonus.
  • Early-career lawyers or paralegals with approximately 2-3 years’ experience in financial services, capital markets, or regulatory practices; fresh graduates will also be considered.
  • Positive attitude, strong work ethic, and willingness to play a supportive role within a wider team.
  • Strong interest in FinTech, financial regulation, payments and derivatives.
  • Good legal research, drafting, and analytical skills.
  • Reliable, detail-oriented, and comfortable in a fast-paced environment.
  • Prior experience in a law firm or in-house financial services team is an advantage.

What We Offer

  • Join a high-calibre senior Singapore legal team within a wider regional and global legal & compliance group.
  • Exposure to complex, cross-border FinTech, payments and financial markets work.
  • Strong learning opportunities and career development.
  • Collaborative, professional team culture that values attitude and contribution.

ESET® provides cutting-edge digital security to prevent attacks before they happen. By combining the power of AI and human expertise, ESET stays ahead of known and emerging cyberthreats — securing businesses, critical infrastructure, and individuals. Whether it’s endpoint, cloud or mobile protection, our AI-native, cloud-first solutions and services remain highly effective and easy to use. Driven by science, ESET technology includes robust detection and response, ultra-secure encryption, and multifactor authentication. With 24/7 real-time defense and strong local support, we keep users safe and businesses running without interruption. An ever-evolving digital landscape demands a progressive, evidence-based approach to security. ESET is committed to world-class scientific research and powerful threat intelligence, backed by R&D centers and a strong global partner network.

ESET's global headquarters are in Bratislava, Slovakia, supporting its worldwide operations. The Asia-Pacific (APAC) region is managed from ESET’s regional headquarters in Singapore, with local offices in Tokyo, Sydney, and the SAARC region and is represented by an extensive global partner network.

Get to know us better – ESET.com, YouTube, Twitter, LinkedIn, Great Place To Work

General Position Overview

In APAC, our fast-growing, multicultural team spans Singapore, India, and Australia—working together across time zones to support customers and colleagues with resilience, integrity, and innovation.
We are now looking for a Systems Support Specialist to join our APAC team. This role is perfect for someone who enjoys solving problems, supporting internal users, and working with business systems (including CRM/Salesforce).

Job Description
Daily Support & User Engagement

  • Provide first-line and second-line support for business systems through tickets, walk-ups, and virtual channels
  • Communicate with end-users to clarify incidents and requests, ensuring timely and accurate resolution
  • Investigate, troubleshoot, and resolve system incidents and user queries

CRM & Systems Operations

  • Support CRM administration (Salesforce experience is a plus)
  • Assist in reviewing, testing, and validating new or modified business systems
  • Develop test data, evaluate results, and provide structured feedback

Documentation & Process Improvement

  • Create, maintain, and distribute user manuals, guides, and support documentation
  • Maintain and enhance system support processes and knowledge bases
  • Monitor and report on incident trends to help improve service quality

Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in Information Systems or a related field
  • 2 years of experience in business systems support or application support
  • Experience with Salesforce or CEM platform would be advantageous
  • Fluent in business English
  • Strong time management and ability to prioritize in a multi-country environment
  • Comfortable working in cross-functional, multicultural teams
  • Reliable, detail-oriented, proactive, and willing to learn

title: Administrative Assistants
raw_description:
Required Qualifications

  • Passionate about the cause of education
  • Dedicated and patient
  • Team spirit
  • Bachelor's Degree in the related subject

Job Timings (Non-negotiable)

  • 44 Hours Per Week
  • Monday - Thursday: 11:30 am - 07:30 pm
  • Saturday and Sunday: 09:30 am - 06:30 pm
  • Lunch Break: 01:30 pm - 02:30 pm

Overtime

Employees who work more than the stipulated working hours on any day will be paid for any additional hour worked at 1.5 times their hourly basic rate of pay.

Public Holidays

  • The Centre will remain closed on all public holidays EXCEPT Labour Day and Vesak Day. The board examinations are held in May/June and we are duty bound to our students to remain open on these two public holidays to assist them in their final examination preparation.
  • Employees who work on Public Holidays (9:30AM - 6:30PM) will receive twice their basic rate of pay for work on that day.

Leave Policy

  • Annual leave : 18 days
  • Birthday Leave: 1 day
  • Family Care Leave : 2 days (the above unused leave will be encashed at the end of the year)
  • Medical leave: 14 days
  • Hospitalisation Leave : 60 days (includes 14 days of medical leave) Unused leaves will lapsed at the end of the year
  • Maternity leave :16 weeks (For Singapore Citizens) 8 weeks (For others)
  • Paternity leave :2 weeks (If employee has worked for 3 continuous months and child is a Singapore Citizen) For Others, as per mutual agreement between employee and employer.
  • Childcare Leave :6 days (if child is less than 7 years, and a Singapore Citizen) 2 days (if child is less than 7 years, for others, as per Employment Act)
  • Compassionate Leave: 3 days (can be availed only once in a year)

Insurance

  • General Physician (GP) : No Co-Pay (Panel Clinic)
  • Specialist (SPL) : $2000 per calendar year (No Panel)
  • Hospitalisation (Hosp) : $60000 Annual (Govt Restr, 4 bedded Ward)
  • Dental Plan (DP) : $1000 per calendar year, 10% Co-Pay (No Panel)

Resignation Procedure

  • Employees are free to resign with just a 1-day notice period. We understand that new opportunities can arise, and we don’t want a lengthy notice period to stand in your way. Our priority is to support your success, whether that’s with us or in your next role.

Walk-in interview

Monday Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Saturday and Sunday at 2:30PM at MathVision Campus, Tekka Place, Annex Block, #01-51 to #01-54 and #01-55 to #01-59, 2 Serangoon Road, Singapore.

title: Admin Associate / Executive (Social Service)
raw_description:
Monday to Friday

8:00 AM – 5:30 PM

Location: Toa Payoh/ Elizabeth Drive

Job Responsibilities

  • Gather social and financial background information of residents and/or their families prior to the application of financial assistance schemes.
  • Update and maintain accurate records in the referral and enquiries database and information system.
  • Ensure timely submission of financial assistance applications such as Medifund, MFEC (Medical Fee Exemption Card), IDAPE (Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly), PGDAS (Pioneer Generation Disability Assistance Scheme), and others.
  • Act as the main point of contact and liaison between residents, families, and healthcare institutions to coordinate referrals and process applications for potential residents.
  • Ensure proper documentation of case files, referrals, and other relevant documents for effective case management and audit purposes.
  • Perform data entry and regularly update information to maintain accuracy of databases.
  • Handle administrative tasks including enquiries, referrals, and admissions.
  • Facilitate residents’ transfers to other facilities or healthcare institutions, as well as coordinate discharge processes.
  • Undertake any other duties or projects as assigned by the supervisor.
  • May required to be stationed in the office to handle incoming phone calls, walk-in enquiries, and other front-desk administrative duties as and when required

Requirements

  • Minimum 1 year of relevant working experience in social service, community service, healthcare, or related sectors.
  • Prior exposure to financial assistance schemes (e.g. Medifund, MFEC, IDAPE, PGDAS) will be an advantage
  • Diploma or Degree in Social Work, Social Sciences, Community Services, Healthcare Administration, or related disciplines preferred.
  • Comfortable liaising with residents, families, healthcare institutions, and external agencies.
  • Strong administrative and documentation skills, with attention to detail for case files and audit purposes.

Candidates pursuing a part-time diploma or degree in a relevant field (e.g. Social Work or Psychology) are welcome to apply


Interested candidates please WhatsApp me at 8779 6100 or send in your resume to willie.goh@swiftsearchglobal.com

We regret to inform you that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

raw_title: Research Associate (Audio LLM) for AI Singapore (Products)
title: Research Associate (Audio LLM) for AI Singapore (Products)
raw_description:
AI Singapore (AISG) is a national AI programme launched by the National Research Foundation (NRF) to anchor deep national capabilities in Artificial Intelligence (AI).

The programme office is hosted by the Nanyang Technological University and brings together all Singapore-based research institutions and the vibrant ecosystem of Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs) in developing AI products to perform use-inspired research, grow the knowledge, create the tools, and develop the talent to power Singapore's AI efforts.

We are looking for a Research Associate for the Audio LLM development efforts of the SEA-LION project under AI Singapore. The successful candidate will help develop and train foundation/large audio language models, build datasets/pipelines for data development, evaluation benchmarks and developer enablement tools for the SEA-LION audio initiatives.

Key Responsibilities:

• Contribute to algorithm design, training and serving SEA-LION team’s LLM audio products.

• Participate in ongoing integration and optimization with entities both in AI Singapore and external collaborators to develop AI Singapore’s LLM solutions.

• Conduct literature review, identify relevant state-of-the-art techniques for multimodal LLMs (audio and text).

• Develop data processing pipeline for multilingual audio datasets.

• Keep abreast of latest developments in NLP, AI and LLMs.

Job Requirements:

• Masters or PhD in computer science, machine learning, AI, and other equivalent relevant fields.

• Familiar with PyTorch, Large Language Model’s Training and Data pipelines.

• Data driven, creative, and resourceful.

• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.

• Able to proactively identify opportunities, take initiative and multi-task in a fast paced environment.

• Background in IT disciplines, working with a wide variety of tools and solutions.

Up-to-date with AI research areas and current trends, especially their application to solving problems.

• Bonus if you have:

• Contribution to past open-source projects.

• Experience as a computer engineer working within a small/medium organization.

• Additional interest in Natural Language Processing (NLP).

We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

raw_title: Senior / Engineer, Data Center Facilities Management (1-Net)
title: Senior / Engineer, Data Center Facilities Management (1-Net)
raw_description:
COMPANY DESCRIPTION

1-Net manages carrier-neutral Internet Data Centers, along with providing a comprehensive range of integrated services including network connectivity, managed services, work area recovery suite, cloud and media delivery services. 1-Net's Data Center Corridor initiative currently interconnects key data centers in Singapore to facilities quick deployment and providing a diverse connectivity options for enterprises and service providers.

1-Net is fast transforming itself into Asia's Data Center of Choice. As one of the pioneers in broadband development in Singapore, 1-Net currently manages telco-class carrier-neutral Internet Data Centers, along with providing domestic and international connectivity, managed services and media delivery services. 1-Net offers superior stability, secure delivery and service experience through its services. It's uniquely positioned in Southeast Asia, with a comprehensive range of integrated services and a media-centric approach to bring cutting edge solutions to its customers.

We are committed to creating an inclusive and diverse workplace where talent thrives. Our hiring decisions are made based on merit and fit-to-role. If you have a disability or special need which requires accommodation to participate in the recruitment process, please inform us when you submit your online application. We will be happy to support as necessary.

Thank you for your interest and application to this role. Please note that only short-listed candidates will be contacted.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Facilities Operation and Maintenance:

  • Conduct the required daily maintenance and operations of the data center facilities to ensure serviceability of all active and redundancy equipment. This includes performing routine facilities activities such as critical system checks and functional testing
  • Respond to Mechanical and Electrical alarms/faults, identify trends, and implement corrective actions as needed
  • Respond and investigate Building Management System (BMS)/ Environmental Monitoring System (EMS) alerts, providing feedback on outstanding issues or events, responding to emergency situations, and being on standby duty as required
  • Support Data Center projects and any additional and alteration work such as site construction activities and installations, coordination with external contractors and testing in adherence to operational standards within the given timeline
  • Other duties as assigned that are consistent with the responsibilities and objectives of the role

Technical and Customer Support:

  • Ensure contracted service level agreements and key performance indicators are met or exceeded
  • Attend to customer requests and deliveries such as installations, coordination with the design & construction, operations requirements to coordinate customer move-in and turn-on, and others

Events and Accreditation Support:

title: Desk Quant Analyst
raw_description:
Squarepoint is a global investment management firm that utilizes a diversified portfolio of systematic and quantitative strategies across financial markets that seeks to achieve high quality, uncorrelated returns for our clients. We have deep expertise in trading, technology and operations and attribute our success to rigorous scientific research. As a technology and data-driven firm, we design and build our own cutting-edge systems, from high performance trading platforms to large scale data analysis and compute farms. With offices around the globe, we emphasize true, global collaboration by aligning our investment, technology and operations teams functionally around the world.


Overview of Desk Quant Analyst Position:

  • Maintain and upgrade the codebase and configuration of strategies within the firm’s automated trading framework.
  • Manage large datasets used in both research and production environments.
  • Perform live as well as historical monitoring of trading activities. For instance, do some post-trade analysis or production reconciliation.
  • Work in close collaboration with quant researchers and traders to understand the constantly evolving needs of the trading desks.

Typical Day of Desk Quant Analyst:

  • Before market open, check that all required data and related processes are ready for the trading day.
  • During market hours, sporadically monitor behavior and performance of strategies.
  • Work on various longer-term projects required for expanding our systematic trading activities and ensuring their robustness.

Skill Set Required For Position:

  • Degree in Engineering, Computer Science or related technical subject.
  • Programming proficiency with at least one major programming or scripting language (e.g. C++, Java, Python).
  • Great attention to details.
  • Strong communication skills and ability to work well with colleagues across multiple regions.
  • Ability to work well under pressure.

Additional Desirable Qualifications:

  • Knowledge of Q/KDB+.
  • Knowledge of Linux and Bash.
  • Advanced quantitative skills.

title: Junior Business Development Executive (Fresh Graduate)
raw_description:
Job description

Salary: 50,000.00 (SGD) - 70,000.00 (SGD) per year


Job description:


International School Group – Singapore HQ Focus: Japan, Thailand & Southeast Asia (SEA) Expansion


Position Overview

We are looking for a motivated fresh graduate to join our Singapore-based international school group as a Junior Business Development Manager. This entry-level role is designed for high-potential talent eager to launch a career in global education expansion, with a focus on Japan, Thailand, and Southeast Asia (SEA).You will support senior leaders in researching new school opportunities, building partnerships, and laying the groundwork for campus launches in high-growth markets. This is a structured 18–24 month accelerated development program with mentorship, regional travel, and clear progression to full Business Development Manager within 2 years.


Location: Singapore Start Date: Q1 2026 Salary: SGD 50,000 – 70,000 per annum + performance bonus (up to 2 months)

Travel: 20–30% (Japan, Thailand, Vietnam, Indonesia, Malaysia)


Key ResponsibilitiesMarket Intelligence & Research

Conduct desktop and field research on education regulations, demographics, and competitor schools in Japan (Tokyo, Osaka), Thailand (Bangkok, Chiang Mai), and SEA cities.


Build and maintain a live database of potential partners: local schools, real estate developers, government bodies, and investor groups.


Partnership Support

Assist in outreach to local education ministries, MOUs, and joint-venture discussions.
Prepare pitch decks, feasibility summaries, and data rooms for senior review.


Project Coordination

Support end-to-end project timelines: from site visits and due diligence to pre-launch marketing plans.
Coordinate with curriculum, admissions, and operations teams in Singapore.


Networking & Representation

Attend education fairs, agent briefings, and regional conferences (e.g., EARCOS, ISAT, BETT Asia) as a junior delegate.
Represent the brand in agent training sessions and parent information events.


Analytics & Reporting

Track KPIs: lead generation, conversion rates, market entry timelines.
Prepare monthly dashboards and board updates using Google Sheets, Tableau, or CRM tools.


Language & Cultural Bridge

Leverage Japanese, Thai, or SEA language skills (if any) to support translation and relationship building.
Participate in cultural immersion trips (e.g., 1-week homestay in rural Thailand or school shadowing in Japan).


Who We’re Looking For (Fresh Graduates)Education

Bachelor’s degree (any discipline) from a recognized university
Preferred majors: Business, International Relations, Education, Marketing, Japanese Studies, Southeast Asian Studies
Final-year GPA: 3.4/4.0 or Second Upper Honours (or equivalent) Experience
No prior full-time work required
Valued (not required):
Internship in education, real estate, consulting, or startup environments
Student leadership (e.g., club president, event organizer)
Study abroad or exchange in Japan, Thailand, or SEA


Skills & Traits

Research & Analytical: Comfortable with Excel, Google Workspace, and basic data visualization
Communication: Clear writer and confident presenter (English fluency required)
Languages (bonus, not mandatory):
Japanese (JLPT N3 or higher)
Thai, Bahasa Indonesia/Malaysia, or Vietnamese
Adaptability: Thrives in ambiguity; eager to learn fast in a dynamic environment
Cultural Curiosity: Genuine interest in Asian education systems and family decision-making


What We Offer

Fast-Track Career: Clear path to mid-level role in 2 years
Regional Exposure: Work and travel across Japan, Thailand, Vietnam, Malaysia
Wellness & Growth:
Annual learning budget (SGD 1,500)
16 days annual leave + 1 study leave day per quarter


How to Apply

Email: careers@kbh.edu.sg

We are an equal opportunity employer. Singapore citizens, PRs, and international graduates with employment pass eligibility are welcome


Job Type: Full-time

Benefits:Health insurance

title: FINANCIAL CONSULTANT
raw_description:
Join one of Singapore’s most dynamic investment firms as it expands its exclusive private client division, in partnership with global investment leaders BlackRock, Fidelity, and Schroders. Backed by a strong private equity heritage, the firm delivers institutional-grade strategies to high-net-worth and ultra-high-net-worth clients seeking access to opportunities typically reserved for large institutions. This position offers a rare platform to craft sophisticated investment solutions, foster enduring client relationships, and contribute to a fast-growing firm redefining wealth management in Asia.

Preferred

  • Recent graduates, Financial Advisors, Relationship Managers, Private Bankers welcomed.
  • A background in investment analysis, portfolio management, or a related field in a private bank, asset management firm, or multi-family office.
  • Demonstrable experience in constructing and managing multi-asset class portfolios, with exposure to equities, fixed income, and alternative investments.
  • Practical experience in interpreting and applying institutional-grade investment research from leading global asset managers to inform strategy.
  • Experience within private banking or wealth management, with a verifiable track record of managing and growing a portfolio of HNW/UHNW relationships. The role is further supported by a consistent stream of qualified leads from the firm's established corporate partnerships.
  • A background that demonstrates exceptional interpersonal skills and the professional gravitas required to build rapport with entrepreneurs, C-suite executives, and multi-generational families.
  • A proven ability to deliver a superior service experience, with outstanding communication skills and a meticulous attention to client service detail.

Required

  • Will need to obtain or posses minimum 4 MAS papers
  • Singaporean Permanent Resident or Citizen only

Marymount/ Bishan

Mon - Fri 9am-6pm

Accounts Payable

  • Posting of vendor invoices into accounting system
  • Checking of external service engineer OT claims prior to submission to recruitment agency for invoice issuance to the Company
  • Generate AP aging report. Reconcile of AP and subledger of GL.

  • Checking of vendor account creation in accounting system (4 eye principle check)
  • Creation of customer account in accounting system
  • Perform various internal controls in accordance with Group & Company’s policy

General Ledger

  • Preparation and posting of recurring and ad hoc journal entries
  • Posting of fixed assets depreciation and direct debit deduction from bank account.
  • Perform bank reconciliation and PO clearing account
  • Maintenance of Fixed Assets Register and perform physical fixed assets sighting and obtain confirmation from the HODs.
  • Preparation and updating of monthly Balance Sheet Reconciliation Schedules (including Fixed Assets Register) with understanding of the transactions in the schedule
  • Follow up on long outstanding transactions in the Balance Sheet Reconciliation Schedules
  • Preparation and submission of monthly cash flow forecast & liquidity report
  • Preparation of department expense (“COO”) report to HODs
  • Support quarterly GST filing (AP side)
  • Ad hoc stock adjustments

-         Generating job costing reports at month end

-         Preparing monthly maintenance report

  • At least 2 years of relevant experience
  • Proficient in MS Office
  • Knowledge of ACCPAC / SAGE300 is advantageous

CEI Reg. No. R23113787

EA License No. 99C4599

raw_title: Assistant / Care Coordinator(237) - National University Polyclinics
title: Assistant / Care Coordinator(237) - National University Polyclinics
raw_description:
As part of the multidisciplinary team, provide holistic and incredible care, empower patients and caregivers to take charge of their own health through technology, raise awareness and accessibility of available resources that can be tapped on, nudging them towards action.

Strategic outcomes and goals include:

  • To build relationships with internal (care team) and external stakeholders (patients and caregivers).
  • To contribute to achieving clinical outcomes for preventive health screenings and reduce readmission and escalations to tertiary care, and, to gather social determinants of health pertinent to influencing social and health outcomes in positive ways
  • Leverage on technology to empower patients to improve health literacy and accessibility to resources
  • Responsible for assisting in coordinating and providing patient care activities. This includes the use of IT systems to track, perform and document established clinical indicators and care given to patient.
  • You will perform basic parameter measurements, close preventive screening and vaccination gaps in relation to national programmes/initiatives.
  • You will also provide basic health education to improve patients’ knowledge on lifestyle management, social and physical activities through social prescribing, the use of health monitoring devices (e.g. home blood pressure, glucose and weight monitoring, as well as advise patients on vaccination requirements.)

Requirement

  • Diploma preferred.
  • Qualification in health promotion will have an advantage.
  • Preferably with some working experience in healthcare
  • Good verbal and written communication skills
  • Pleasant disposition and personality
  • Possess good interpersonal skills, a positive attitude towards work and is a team player.
  • Good communication and coordination skills
  • Self-motivated and has interest in patient care.
  • Able to communicate in English and a local language/dialect.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office including Excel, Words and Power Point
  • Savvy in navigating IT programs

title: VETERINARY NURSE / TECHNICIAN
raw_description:
Job Description

  • Conduct diagnostic tests, laboratory procedures, and radiographic imaging (X-rays) as required

  • Prepare animals, surgical instruments, and treatment areas for surgical and medical procedures

  • Administer medications, vaccinations, anaesthesia, and treatments under the supervision of a Veterinarian

  • Provide emergency first aid, inpatient nursing care, and post-operative monitoring

  • Observe, assess, and accurately report on animals’ behaviour, vital signs, and clinical condition

  • Safely restrain animals for examination, treatment, and diagnostic procedures

  • Assist veterinarians during consultations, surgeries, and medical procedures

  • Collect, document, and maintain accurate animal case histories and medical records

  • Ensure compliance with clinical protocols, hygiene standards, and workplace safety procedures

Job Requirements

  • Minimum Degree in Veterinary Medicine

  • At least 2 years of relevant working experience in a veterinary clinic, hospital, or related animal care setting

  • Able to work independently with minimal supervision, as well as collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team

  • Strong sense of responsibility, professionalism, and ethical conduct

  • Demonstrates a caring, calm, and positive disposition when handling animals and interacting with colleagues or clients

  • Maintains strict confidentiality of medical records and client information

  • Committed to continuous professional development and keeping up to date with veterinary best practices

  • Open-minded, adaptable, and willing to learn new techniques and protocols

  • Able to prioritise work based on clinical urgency, presenting cases, and emergency situations.

  • Willing to work flexible hours when required to meet operational and emergency care needs

raw_title: Software Quality Assurance & Testing Analyst [Automation & Manual Testing; SIT; UAT; PROD] (Banking Wealth Management Application)
title: Software Quality Assurance & Testing Analyst [Automation & Manual Testing; SIT; UAT; PROD] (Banking Wealth Management Application)
raw_description:
Project: Wealth Management System Implementation - Bank Sector Client.

You will play a crucial role in driving testing efforts for the programme. This includes defining, developing, documenting, executing and reporting on test approaches and plans; collaborating closely with project teams as well as other relevant stakeholders such as end users, subject matter experts, technical teams etc.


Job Description

Your role will require close collaboration across multiple areas within the organization including but not limited to:

1. Project Management Office (PMO)

2. Information Technology (IT)

3. Product Development & Strategy (PD&S)

4. Business Units / Divisions (BU/Ds)

5. Operations (Ops)

6. Risk Management

8. Other relevant units as needed


You will need to develop expertise in understanding the business processes and functions supported by the new system as well as conduct detailed reviews of user requirements and specifications. You will also need to develop and execute test plans, procedures and scenarios to ensure that the system meets all requirements and works seamlessly with other systems.


Responsibilities:

• Define, develop, document, execute and report on test approaches and plans.

• Collaborate closely with project teams as well as other relevant stakeholders such as end users, subject matter experts, technical teams etc.

• Develop and maintain comprehensive test plans for each release, which cover functional and non-functional requirements.

• Design and implement automated and manual test scripts for both short-term and long-term needs.

• Conduct unit, integration and performance tests using various tools and frameworks.

• Provide regular status updates on testing progress and issues.

• Identify and track software defects and work with developers to resolve them.

• Ensure compliance with quality standards and best practices throughout the testing process.

• Support user acceptance testing (UAT) activities.

• Execute ad hoc queries and perform exploratory testing as needed.



Qualifications:

• Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field.

• Minimum 3 years of experience in IT testing methodologies, tools and techniques.

• Experience in designing and implementing test automation solutions.

• Familiarity with Agile development methodologies.

• Good communication skills and ability to interact effectively with cross-functional teams.

• Ability to analyze complex problems and propose innovative solutions.

• Excellent attention to detail and strong organizational skills.



Good To Have:

- Automation Software Testing: TOSCA; Selenium; Appium

- Jira; Confluence: AS400 Test Data Preparation; AS400 Host Data; SQL Query

- Domain Knowledge: Unit Trusts; Equities; Forex; Wealth Portfolio Management; Insurance; CRM MS365 Dynamics; Sibel CRM Application

title: Manufacturing Engineer
raw_description:
JOB SUMMARY

Duties include generating PCR and engineering drawing and exploring alternate solution to solve resource or production constrain. He is also responsible to plan, organize and manage assigned project activities. This requires direct interaction with customer, vendors and functional departments to monitor and ascertaining all jobs are progressing within schedule and meeting deadline.

RESPONSIBILITIES & DUTIES

  • Understand the full requirements of all manufacturing and improvement project. Follow up on the kick start, execution, monitoring and work closely with related personnel to resolve issues until the completion of the project.
  • Perform technical review on sales order and work closely with the sales department or customer to clarify all technical specifications and job requirements on the sales order.
  • Generate PCR and manufacturing process plan.
  • Generate engineering drawings and perform drafting.
  • Support Asst. Engineering Manager on the job costing.
  • Continuously explore for new manufacturing method or new external providers to support the production.
  • Work closely with the Production Supervisor to resolve any production issue.
  • Support the procurement department to deal with external providers on all technical issues.
  • Understand and be aware of the impact of each activity to the company, its environment and minimize any detrimental results.
  • Follow safe work practices and procedures.
  • Ensure company and QHSE policies and procedures are adhered to at all times.
  • Participate/ attend all functions, activities and meeting organized by the company.
  • Perform other assignment and duties as and when required.

ACCOUNTABILITIES

  • Ensure on time issuance of PCR and drawings and correctness.
  • Ensure all projects are on schedule and completed on time.
  • Work with the team to achieve QHSE objectives.
  • Maintain good housekeeping at all times.

QUALIFICATION & EXPERIENCE

  • Degree preferably in Manufacturing/ Mechanical Engineering discipline or equivalent.
  • Preferably 2 years of relevant experience in the metal machining or oil and gas industries.

title: Research Intern
raw_description:
We are looking for a meticulous, diligent, and resourceful individual to evaluate the impact of sustainability projects. The intern will analyse case studies according to the Steward Leadership framework and identify scalable, sustainable projects that address environmental and social challenges.

Responsibilities:

  • Review case studies: Thoroughly evaluate all materials and present findings to the reviewing team.
  • Conduct desktop research on impact metrics, existing regulations, and industry standards to perform robust analysis of case studies.
  • Collaborate with manager and teammates: Work closely with the process manager and teammates to ensure smooth communication and adherence to timelines.
  • Assist in other administrative and research development duties as needed.

Requirements:

  • Postgraduate students from a relevant field such as Social Science, Business Administration, Corporate Sustainability, or Sustainable Development are preferred.
  • Attention to detail: Keen eye for spotting outstanding qualities in case studies and ensuring compliance with guidelines.
  • Time management skills: Ability to meet deadlines and manage the review process efficiently.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Good team player yet able to work well independently.
  • Meticulous, analytical, highly organised and intellectually inquisitive.
  • Prior experience in evaluating case studies is a bonus.

Application deadline: 30 April 2026

Internship Period: 18 May – 25 Jun 26, full time.

Location: Singapore; Hybrid (you are required to attend meetings in-person when necessary)

To apply: Please submit your resume and a brief cover letter explaining your relevant experience to adelinefoo@stewardshipasia.com.sg.

We look forward to your application!

title: Operations Executive (Air Freight Export and Import)
raw_description:
Operations Executive, Air Export/Import

Location: Alps Avenue

You will support import/export air freight operations by managing bookings, coordinating cargo movement, preparing documentation, and ensuring compliance with safety and regulatory standards (e.g., IATA, RACAR). You'll be expected to liaise with customers and overseas partners to meet service excellence standards.

Principal Duties and Responsibilities

• Arrange shipment collection/traffic co-ordination

• Consolidation planning and trouble shooting

• Ensure that export/import job files are checked in system and supplier invoices

are registered as passed promptly. Responsible to complete the job process.

• Ensure that pre-alerts are sent in a timely manner to customers

• Execute export documentation, customs formalities, invoicing, cargo canvassing and bookings.

• Cargo Reservation. Place booking with airlines and/or co-loaders.

• Responding to overseas offices/partners e-mail

• To assist in ensuring safety and security compliance by stopping any suspicious cargo for further process and report to department supervisor/manager immediately. All Dangerous Goods to be handled in accordance with IATA Rules & Regulations.

• To prepare invoices with accurate charges to customers on a timely manner

• Adhere to the security procedures as per Regulated Air Cargo Agent Security Programme (RACAR)

• Any other duties assigned by the superiors. Including carry out duty after normal working hours, public holidays, on site job.

Requirements:

  • Minimum “O” Levels or equivalent

  • Strong experience in Air Export operation and document with basic air import clearance knowledge.

  • Minimum 3 years in air freight operations handling export/import activities.

  • Fresh graduates are welcome to apply

  • Effective Interpersonal skills

  • Experience in Air Freight operations and documentation.

  • Willing to work on PH and weekends due to operational exigencies.

  • Able to work under tight timeline.

  • Possess high integrity and strong commitment.

  • Good knowledge of documentation of import/export procedures with interest in continuous learning.

  • A good team player and yet able to work independently.

  • At least 1–3 years of experience in air freight operations (export/import)

  • Good knowledge of customs and air cargo procedures

  • Willingness to work on weekends or PHs if required

If you’re keen to be part of a trusted global brand in shipping and logistics, apply now!
Please indicate which position you are applying for (Sea Freight or Air Freight), and include your last drawn salary and expected salary in your application.

📍 Please note: Air Freight role is based at 70 Alps Avenue;

title: Social Worker, Residential Services
raw_description:
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES AND ACCOUNTABILITIES

A. FUNCTIONAL

· Case management and counselling of boys admitted under Child Protection Orders, Probation Orders, Family Guidance Orders MSF-referred cases and Compassionate Ground cases.

· Carry out admission procedures for boys admitted to Boys’ Town, which includes handling intake interviews and liaising with stakeholders working with the boys prior to admission.

· Conduct assessment of identified needs through assessment tools such as Child and Adolescent Needs and Strengths (CANS) and Youth Level of Service/Case Management Inventory (YLS/CMI).

· Conduct sessions for the residents of Boys’ Town and their parents/caregivers according to their identified needs.

· Develop an Individualised Care Plan (ICP) for all residents admitted. As part of this ICP, you will be required to work closely with other internal and external stakeholders to ensure holistic care for the residents.

· Initiate and lead internal case reviews with the support of your clinical supervisor and Social Work Lead. Ensure that a periodic assessment of our residents’ progress in Boys’ Town is conducted.

· Provide timely reports on case progress to the Social Work Lead in and bring up cases for supervision accordingly. To undergo supervision with assigned supervisor on a monthly basis.

· Keep timely case records and ensure proper documentation of residents’ personal information and reports. (eg. Case notes, court reports, review reports, ICP, etc). Ensure the licensing audit requirements are met in the case management domain as advised by Head of Home.

· Represent the residents and Boys’ Town professionally in various platforms such as Court Hearings, MSF Reviews, School conferences, etc with the support of clinical supervisor.

· Conduct psychoeducational and therapeutic group work programmes as advised by Social Work Lead and/ or Head of Home.

· Liaise with Education Officers and schools which the residents are studying in and keep them informed of admission, discharge, and absence of the residents from any school programmes, as well as coming up with their education care plan.

· Work in collaboration with the Residential Services Leads, operations team and Education Officers in monitoring the progress of the residents, through the sharing of case related issues and intervention plans.

· Liaise with external agencies eg. Child Protection Officers, Probation Officers, Social Workers, Family Service Centres, Police for case management purposes and to support the case plans for the residents.

· Work in collaboration with the Programmes/Operations departments in organising annual camps for the residents. Work in close collaboration with the other staff of the Home and of the other components of Boys’ Town in matters pertaining to the welfare of our residents.

· Support in conducting research for Boys’ Town as necessary.

· Maintain complete confidentiality of all information pertaining to the residents of Boys’ Town.

· Other than the above, the Directors and/or Executive Director may assign you any other duties that are within reason for the benefit of the clients, and/or Boys’ Town.


B. ORGANIZATIONAL

· To participate actively in all fundraising activities as well as any other commitments that Boys’ Town undertakes.

· To work in close collaboration with other staff, departments and components of Boys’ Town.

· The Executive Director and or his/her representative may assign you any other duties that are within reason for the operational needs of Boys’ Town


EXPERIENCE AND QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS

· Bachelor / Master of Social Work OR other recognised Social Work qualifications from Singapore Association of Social Workers (SASW).

· Relevant experience in VWO/ NPO/ social service sector with direct experience in working with vulnerable children and youths and their families is desirable
